title: known MVP - Memory Graph for LLMs
description: Build a memory graph system for LLMs with: vector similarity search, graph traversal, hierarchical scopes, conflict detection, and LLM-first resolution. PostgreSQL+pgvector backend, pluggable embeddings (local-first), CLI interface.
design:
## Architectural Decisions

### Module Structure
- Single Go module: github.com/dustin/known (or similar)
- Packages: model/, storage/, embed/, query/, cmd/

### Configuration
- Viper for configuration
- Config file: ~/.known/config.yaml
- Environment variable overrides

### IDs
- ULID for all entity IDs (sortable, URL-safe)

### Embedding Dimensions
- Stored per-entry to support mixed embedding models
- Allows migration between models without re-embedding everything

### Predefined Edge Types
- depends-on, contradicts, supersedes, elaborates, related-to
- Extensible via edge_types table

### Scope Model
- Hierarchical paths (e.g., 'project.auth.oauth')
- Scoped queries stay within scope unless explicitly traversing links
- No implicit parent inheritance

### Conflict Resolution
- LLM-first: surface conflicts with metadata for calling model to decide
- Stable confidence metadata (source-based, not LLM-generated)
- ResolutionStatus: unresolved | resolved | superseded
close_reason: MVP complete: all 6 subtasks delivered — core model, PostgreSQL+pgvector storage, pluggable embeddings, vector+graph+hybrid query engine, concurrency hardening, and full CLI with 15 commands.

title: Core Knowledge Model
description: Define Go structs for the knowledge graph primitives
design:
## Data Structures

### Entry (knowledge unit)
- ID (ULID)
- Content (text, user-defined granularity)
- Embedding ([]float32 + dimension)
- Source (provenance: file path, URL, conversation ID, etc.)
- SourceMeta (line numbers, timestamps, etc.)
- Confidence (stable metadata, not LLM-generated)
- CreatedAt, UpdatedAt
- TTL (optional expiration)
- Scope (hierarchical path)
- Custom metadata (extensible key-value)

### Edge (relationship)
- ID (ULID)
- FromID, ToID (ULID refs)
- Type (predefined + extensible)
- Weight (optional)
- Metadata (extensible)
- CreatedAt

### Predefined Edge Types
- depends-on: X requires Y
- contradicts: X conflicts with Y (for conflict detection)
- supersedes: X replaces Y (newer version)
- elaborates: X provides detail about Y
- related-to: generic association

### Scope
- Path (hierarchical, e.g., 'project.auth.oauth')
- Inheritance rules: queries stay scoped unless explicitly following links

## Conflict Metadata
- ConflictsWith []EntryID
- ResolutionStatus: unresolved | resolved | superseded
- LastVerified timestamp
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Go structs defined: Entry, Edge, Scope, EdgeType
- [ ] ULID generation for all IDs
- [ ] Predefined edge types as typed constants (depends-on, contradicts, supersedes, elaborates, related-to)
- [ ] Extensible metadata field (map[string]any or typed wrapper)
- [ ] Embedding dimension stored per-entry (supports mixed models)
- [ ] JSON serialization/deserialization working
- [ ] Validation methods (e.g., scope path format, required fields)
- [ ] Unit tests for all structs and validation
close_reason: All acceptance criteria met. Core model implemented with Entry, Edge, Scope, EdgeType structs. ULID-based IDs, predefined edge types, extensible metadata, per-entry embedding dimensions, JSON serialization, and validation methods. 30 unit tests passing.

title: Storage Layer - PostgreSQL + pgvector
description: Implement PostgreSQL storage with pgvector for embeddings and ltree for hierarchical scopes
design:
## Schema Design

### Tables
- entries: core knowledge storage with vector column
- edges: relationship graph
- scopes: scope metadata and configuration
- edge_types: registry of valid edge types (predefined + custom)

### Key Features
- pgvector for similarity search (cosine, L2, inner product)
- ltree for hierarchical scope paths
- GIN indexes for metadata JSONB queries
- Partial indexes for active (non-expired) entries

## Interface
- Repository pattern with interfaces for testability
- Connection pooling (pgxpool)
- Migration support (golang-migrate or similar)

## Queries to Support
- Vector similarity within scope
- Graph traversal (BFS/DFS)
- Conflict detection (find contradicts edges)
- TTL expiration cleanup
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] PostgreSQL schema with migrations (golang-migrate)
- [ ] pgvector column for embeddings (dimension from entry)
- [ ] ltree column for hierarchical scope paths
- [ ] Repository interfaces: EntryRepo, EdgeRepo, ScopeRepo
- [ ] CRUD operations for all entities
- [ ] Vector similarity search (cosine) within scope
- [ ] Basic adjacency queries (edges in/out)
- [ ] TTL expiration cleanup function
- [ ] Connection pooling with pgxpool
- [ ] Integration tests with testcontainers
close_reason: All files implemented: repository interfaces, PostgreSQL impl with pgvector/ltree, migrations, integration tests. Compiles clean, integration tests gated by KNOWN_INTEGRATION=1.

title: Embedding Layer - Pluggable Local-First
description: Create pluggable embedding interface with local-first implementation (Ollama) and OpenAI-compatible adapter
design:
## Interface Design

type Embedder interface {
    Embed(ctx context.Context, text string) ([]float32, error)
    EmbedBatch(ctx context.Context, texts []string) ([][]float32, error)
    Dimensions() int
    ModelName() string
}

## Implementations

### OllamaEmbedder (default, local-first)
- Uses Ollama API (localhost:11434)
- Supports models: nomic-embed-text, mxbai-embed-large, etc.
- Configurable model selection

### OpenAICompatibleEmbedder
- Works with any OpenAI-compatible API
- Configurable base URL, API key, model
- Supports: OpenAI, Azure OpenAI, local servers (vLLM, etc.)

## Configuration
- KNOWN_EMBEDDER=ollama|openai-compatible
- KNOWN_EMBED_MODEL=nomic-embed-text
- KNOWN_EMBED_URL=http://localhost:11434
- KNOWN_EMBED_API_KEY=(for remote APIs)

## Caching
- Optional embedding cache to avoid re-embedding identical content
- Content hash → embedding mapping
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Embedder interface defined (Embed, EmbedBatch, Dimensions, ModelName)
- [ ] OllamaEmbedder implementation working
- [ ] OpenAICompatibleEmbedder implementation working
- [ ] Viper configuration for embedder selection and settings
- [ ] Returns dimension with embeddings for storage
- [ ] Graceful error handling (connection failures, model not found)
- [ ] Unit tests with mocked HTTP responses
- [ ] Integration test with real Ollama (skippable if not available)
close_reason: Embedder interface with Ollama and OpenAI-compatible implementations. SHA-256 caching, Viper config, 24 unit tests passing.

title: Query Engine - Vector + Graph + Hybrid
description: Implement query engine supporting vector similarity, graph traversal, and hybrid queries with conflict detection
design:
## Query Types

### Vector Search
- Similarity search within scope
- Top-K with threshold
- Recency weighting option
- Exclusion filters (NOT containing X)

### Graph Traversal  
- Directional: outgoing edges (what does X depend on?)
- Directional: incoming edges (what depends on X?)
- Transitive closure (N hops from X)
- Filter by edge type
- Path finding between entries

### Hybrid Queries
- Vector search + graph expansion
- 'Find similar to X, then expand relationships'
- 'Find everything in scope related to topic within N hops'

### Conflict Detection
- Find entries with 'contradicts' edges
- Surface unresolved conflicts for LLM decision
- Return conflict metadata with query results

## Result Format
- Entry with full metadata
- Provenance chain (source info)
- Conflict flags
- Relevance/similarity scores
- Relationship context (how was this reached?)
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Vector similarity search with scope filtering and top-K
- [ ] Recency weighting option for similarity results
- [ ] Exclusion filters (NOT matching X)
- [ ] Graph traversal: outgoing edges from entry
- [ ] Graph traversal: incoming edges to entry
- [ ] Transitive closure (N hops) with edge type filtering
- [ ] Hybrid query: vector search + graph expansion
- [ ] Conflict detection: surface entries with contradicts edges
- [ ] Results include: entry, provenance, similarity score, conflict flags, path info
- [ ] Integration tests for all query types
close_reason: Query engine implemented with vector search (cosine/L2/inner product metric-aware scoring), BFS graph traversal (directional, depth-limited, cycle-protected, edge-type filtered), hybrid vector+graph expansion, conflict detection, and FindPath. All review fixes applied: metric-aware distanceToScore, sort-before-truncate for recency, robust edge type filtering, correct buildPathResults for incoming edges, proper error propagation.

comments.author: Dustin
comments.text: Code review completed. 1 CRITICAL (distanceToScore ignores metric), 3 HIGH (recency re-sort after limit, edge type filter fragility, buildPathResults wrong start node for incoming edges), 6 MEDIUM (N+1 queries, no global search, naming, missing seed/via). Creating follow-up bead for fixes.

title: CLI Interface
description: Build CLI tool for interacting with the knowledge graph (similar to beads UX)
design:
## Commands

### Knowledge Management
- known add 'content' --scope=X --source=Y --tags=a,b
- known update <id> --content=... --confidence=...
- known delete <id>
- known show <id> (full entry with relationships)

### Querying
- known search 'query' --scope=X --limit=10
- known related <id> --depth=2 --edge-type=depends-on
- known conflicts [--scope=X] (show unresolved conflicts)

### Graph Operations
- known link <from> <to> --type=depends-on
- known unlink <from> <to>
- known path <from> <to> (find connection)

### Scope Management
- known scope list
- known scope create <path>
- known scope tree (show hierarchy)

### Maintenance
- known gc (clean expired entries)
- known stats [--scope=X]
- known export --format=json|jsonl
- known import <file>

## Output Formats
- Human-readable (default)
- JSON (--json flag)
- Quiet mode for scripting (--quiet)
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Commands: add, update, delete, show (knowledge management)
- [ ] Commands: search, related, conflicts (querying)
- [ ] Commands: link, unlink, path (graph operations)
- [ ] Commands: scope list/create/tree (scope management)
- [ ] Commands: gc, stats, export, import (maintenance)
- [ ] Viper config file support (~/.known/config.yaml)
- [ ] --json flag for machine-readable output
- [ ] --quiet flag for scripting
- [ ] Helpful error messages and --help for all commands
- [ ] Basic integration tests for key workflows
close_reason: Full CLI implemented: 4 CRUD commands (add/update/delete/show) + 11 query/graph/maintenance commands (search/related/conflicts/path/link/unlink/scope/gc/stats/export/import). Two parallel agents built on separate worktrees, merged with conflict resolution. Infrastructure: signal context, lazy embedder init, Viper config, human/JSON/quiet output modes.

title: Concurrency hardening for multiagent use
description: Harden the storage layer for concurrent access by multiple LLM agents writing to the same knowledge graph simultaneously.
design:
## Problems Identified

### 1. Lost Updates (Critical)
Update() does a blind UPDATE WHERE id=$1 with no version check. Two agents modifying the same entry causes last-write-wins silently.

Fix: Add version column to entries table, check in WHERE clause on update. Return a typed ConcurrentModificationError so callers can retry.

### 2. No Transaction Support (High)
Repo interfaces have no way to group operations atomically. Edge creation itself runs two separate Exec calls. Entry + edges can't be created atomically.

Fix: Add WithTx(ctx, func(tx Tx) error) to DB layer. Transaction-aware repos that accept a tx parameter or use a context-embedded transaction.

### 3. Duplicate Knowledge / TOCTOU (Medium)  
Search-then-create has a race window. Two agents analyzing the same content create near-duplicate entries.

Fix: Content-hash column with UNIQUE constraint (or unique on hash+scope). Upsert semantics for idempotent writes.

### 4. Scope FK Without Auto-Creation (Medium)
entries.scope REFERENCES scopes(path) means insert fails if scope doesn't exist. Concurrent agents racing to populate a new scope area hit FK violations.

Fix: Auto-upsert scope hierarchy on entry creation (within the same transaction).

## Migration Required
- 002_concurrency.up.sql: add version column, content_hash column, unique constraint
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Version column on entries, incremented on update
- [ ] Update returns ConcurrentModificationError on version mismatch
- [ ] WithTx pattern on DB for multi-operation atomicity
- [ ] Content hash column with unique constraint per scope
- [ ] Entry creation auto-upserts scope hierarchy
- [ ] Edge creation wrapped in single transaction
- [ ] Migration 002 for schema changes
- [ ] Tests for concurrent modification detection
- [ ] Tests for duplicate entry prevention
close_reason: Concurrency hardening applied: optimistic locking with version column, content hash dedup with UNIQUE(content_hash, scope), atomic Create/CreateOrUpdate via auto-transactions, EnsureHierarchy for scope FK safety, WithTx support. Review fixes: RETURNING-based update for TOCTOU elimination, ActualVersion populated, ErrDuplicateContent sentinel detection, pgcrypto removed for PG14+ builtin sha256.

title: Unify scoring across vector and expansion results
description: Graph-expanded entries in SearchHybrid get edge.EffectiveWeight() as their score, which measures relationship strength — not query relevance. This means a weight-1.0 edge to an irrelevant topic outranks a 0.8 edge to a relevant one. Vector results and expansion results exist on incompatible scales, making the combined result set poorly ranked. Replace edge.EffectiveWeight() with parentScore * edgeWeight * depthDecay so all results are commensurable and sortable. Location: query/hybrid.go:146
acceptance_criteria: Expansion results scored as parentScore * edgeWeight * depthDecayFactor. Combined vector + expansion results sorted by unified score. Tests cover scoring consistency across direct and expanded results.

title: Design testbed to show tool effectiveness
description: Design a testbed/benchmark that demonstrates known's effectiveness as an LLM memory tool — proving recall quality, session continuity, or other measurable benefits. GH#14.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Testbed design document exists with methodology. 2. At least one quantitative metric is defined (e.g. recall accuracy, task completion rate). 3. The testbed can be run reproducibly.

title: Trim discover skill redundant tool guidance
description: Discover skill includes basic tool usage like 'ls the root directory' and redundant scope/CLI instructions already in always-on context. The phased structure and opinionated guidance is intentional and should stay, but basic tool instructions should be removed.
acceptance_criteria: 1. No basic tool usage instructions (ls, cat, etc.) in discover skill 2. Phased structure preserved 3. Scope creation and known CLI examples remain (these are specific to discover workflow)
close_reason: Removed 'ls the root directory' instruction from discover skill Phase 1. Softened '2-5 files' rule to 'Read enough files to understand purpose — usually 2-5'. Changed 'Extract one atomic fact' to 'Extract atomic facts'. Kept phased structure and all opinionated quality/density guidance intact.

title: Fix runInit signature: interface{} should be context.Context
description: runInit in cmd/init.go:18 takes interface{} as first parameter instead of context.Context, unlike every other run* function. Inconsistent and will need changing if init ever needs context.
acceptance_criteria: runInit signature matches other run* functions: func runInit(ctx context.Context, args []string) error

title: Improve tag guidance and QoL
description: Tags are supported metadata but have poor discoverability and unknown retrieval impact. Users can't easily list, search, or recall by tag. Need to surface tag functionality and ensure it affects retrieval. GH#23.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Users can list all tags in use. 2. Tags can be used as a filter in search/recall. 3. Plugin guidance mentions tags where appropriate. 4. Tag impact on retrieval is documented.
close_reason: Already fixed in ec51edd — added 'known label list', dynamic shell completion for --label flags, and scaffold docs for label discoverability.

title: Add timeouts to HTTP clients in embedder implementations
description: OllamaEmbedder (embed/ollama.go:36) and OpenAICompatibleEmbedder (embed/openai.go:40) create http.Client{} with no Timeout. A slow or unresponsive embedding service can hang the CLI indefinitely. The context cancellation from signal.NotifyContext only fires on SIGINT, not on duration.
acceptance_criteria: Both embedder HTTP clients have a reasonable default timeout (e.g. 30s). Integration test in embed/integration_test.go already uses 2s timeout as reference.

title: Add FTS as a third retrieval signal
description: The system has zero BM25 or full-text search capability. Exact matches on proper nouns, version numbers, IP addresses, or technical identifiers (e.g., "pgvector 0.7.4", "OIDC", "10.0.1.5") rely entirely on the embedding model. For a memory system storing environment facts and configuration details, this is a meaningful gap. Add FTS5 (SQLite) or tsvector (Postgres) keyword search and combine results via reciprocal rank fusion (RRF) for true hybrid search. Location: storage/storage.go (new SearchKeyword method)
acceptance_criteria: SQLite backend supports FTS5 full-text search. Postgres backend supports tsvector search. Results from keyword search are combined with vector results via RRF or weighted fusion. Tests verify exact-match queries (identifiers, IPs, version numbers) return correct results even when embedding similarity is low.

title: Hybrid search: fuse vector and BM25 results with RRF
description:
Vector and BM25 scores are on incompatible scales (cosine: 0.4-0.9, BM25 via 1/(1+|rank|): 0.1-0.3). The current hybrid merge takes the higher score per entry, so vector results almost always win and text-only matches rank below mediocre vector matches. Need proper rank fusion so both retrieval methods contribute meaningfully.

RRF (Reciprocal Rank Fusion) is the standard approach: score = sum(1/(k + rank_position)) across result lists. It ignores raw scores entirely, using only rank position, so no calibration needed. Alternative: min-max normalization + weighted blend.
design: RRF (Reciprocal Rank Fusion) replaces MAX fusion. Score = sum(1/(k + rank)) across vector and text result lists. k=60 (standard default, configurable via HybridOptions.RRFk). Raw scores from vector (cosine) and text (BM25) are discarded — only rank position matters, so scale incompatibility is irrelevant. Recall command now always runs text+vector hybrid (TextSearch: true). Text threshold zeroed in hybrid path since BM25 scores shouldn't be filtered by cosine threshold. Reach field attributed to whichever method ranked the entry higher.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Hybrid search (--text --hybrid or TextSearch=true) produces a single merged ranking where text-only matches can outrank weak vector matches
2. Score fusion uses rank position (RRF) rather than raw scores
3. Default similarity threshold works correctly for fused results (no silent filtering)
4. Existing pure --text and pure vector paths unchanged
5. Test: entry with exact keyword match but poor embedding similarity ranks higher than entry with good embedding but no keyword match
close_reason: RRF fusion implemented and merged in PR #26. fuseByRRF replaces MAX fusion using rank-position scoring (1/(k+rank)). Text search is best-effort for backend compatibility. Recall enables text+vector hybrid by default.

title: SIMD-friendly distance computation
description: The cosineDistance and l2Distance functions in storage/sqlite/vec.go promote every float32 element to float64 in a scalar loop, preventing compiler auto-vectorization. Investigate: (1) operating on float32 natively (sufficient precision for ranking), (2) loop structure that enables SIMD auto-vectorization by the Go compiler, (3) manual SIMD via assembly or cgo (e.g. using x86 AVX2/ARM NEON intrinsics). Also consider pre-computing and storing vector norms so cosine distance becomes a single dot product plus a division.
acceptance_criteria: Distance computation is measurably faster (>2x) on benchmark dimensions 384/768/1536 compared to baseline from known-hrz. Correctness verified by existing tests.

title: Add HTTP timeouts and batch chunking to embedding providers
description: Both the Ollama and OpenAI-compatible embedding backends create &http.Client{} with no timeout — a hung server blocks indefinitely. The OpenAI-compatible EmbedBatch sends all texts in a single HTTP request with no size limit, but the OpenAI API has a limit of 2048 inputs per batch. Large batches will fail with HTTP 400. No retry logic or rate limiting exists for transient failures. Location: embed/openai.go:41, embed/ollama.go:37, embed/openai.go:81-86
acceptance_criteria: HTTP clients have configurable timeout (default 30s). EmbedBatch chunks requests to a configurable max batch size (default 256). Tests verify timeout behavior. Tests verify batch chunking produces correct results.

title: Adapt hooks for plugin format
description: Convert the SessionStart hook from the current settings.json format to the plugin hooks format. The hook runs 'known scope tree' to inject scope context at session start. Verify the plugin hook schema matches what Claude Code expects from plugins (hooks/ directory with JSON config).
acceptance_criteria:
1. plugin/hooks/ contains valid hook configuration
2. SessionStart hook runs 'known scope tree' with graceful fallback if known not installed
3. Hook format matches Claude Code plugin hook spec (not settings.json format)
close_reason: Hooks defined inline in plugin.json SessionStart section. Command: 'command -v known >/dev/null && known scope tree 2>/dev/null || true' — graceful fallback when binary missing. Matches beads plugin pattern (no separate hooks/ directory needed).

title: Standardize ErrNotFound comparison to errors.Is()
description: storage.ErrNotFound is a sentinel error compared with == in some places (e.g. cmd/scope.go:129) and errors.Is() in others (e.g. query/graph.go:113). The == comparisons will break silently if any storage implementation wraps the error.
acceptance_criteria: All comparisons to storage.ErrNotFound use errors.Is(). No == comparisons remain. Grep confirms.

title: Migrate SQLite driver from ncruces/go-sqlite3 (WASM) to modernc.org/sqlite
description: The ncruces/go-sqlite3 driver compiles SQLite to WASM, adding ~1.5s startup cost to every CLI invocation that touches the DB. This makes shell completions (and all DB commands) unacceptably slow. modernc.org/sqlite is a pure-Go transpilation with near-zero startup overhead and is the standard CGo-free SQLite driver in the Go ecosystem.
acceptance_criteria:
1. go.mod references modernc.org/sqlite, not ncruces/go-sqlite3
2. All existing tests pass (go test ./...)
3. known __complete scopes runs in <100ms
4. known list --limit 1 runs in <200ms
5. No CGo dependency introduced
6. storage/sqlite package uses the modernc driver registration name
7. DSN/URI format updated if modernc requires different format than ncruces
8. Any documentation referencing the SQLite driver is updated
close_reason: Migration completed: ncruces/go-sqlite3 (WASM) replaced with modernc.org/sqlite (pure-Go transpilation). All tests pass, no CGo dependency introduced.

title: Add 'list' command for browsing entries without search query or ULID
description:
All current retrieval paths require either a ULID (show, related, path, delete) or a semantic query (search, recall). There's no way to simply browse entries. This feature covers three changes:

1. New 'known list' command for browsing with filters (scope, source-type, confidence, limit)
2. Make 'recall' work without a query — 'known recall --scope storage' dumps all entries in that scope in the plain-text LLM-friendly format
3. Make 'show' accept a scope — 'known show --scope storage' shows all entries in that scope with full detail (edges, metadata)

The common thread: every retrieval command should work with scope as an alternative to ULID or query string.
acceptance_criteria:
1. 'known list' shows all entries with summary output (ID, truncated content, scope, confidence, age)
2. 'known list --scope storage' filters to entries in that scope and descendants
3. 'known list --source-type conversation' filters by source type
4. 'known list --confidence verified' filters by confidence level
5. 'known list --limit N' caps output (default reasonable, e.g. 20)
6. Filters are composable (e.g. --scope storage --confidence verified)
7. Output is human-readable by default, with --json for programmatic use
8. 'known recall --scope storage' (no query arg) returns all entries in scope using recall's plain-text format
9. 'known show --scope storage' returns all entries in scope with full detail including edges
10. All three commands support --limit
close_reason: Added 'list' command with --scope/--source-type/--confidence/--limit/--json filters. Made recall work without query when --scope provided. Made show accept --scope as alternative to ULID. Added SourceType filter to EntryFilter in storage layer.

title: Fix scope resolution: bare dotted notation fails in recall/search
description: recall and search return no results with --scope copilot or --scope copilot.auth, but work with --scope /copilot. Bare scope names are interpreted relative to auto-detected CWD scope (e.g. the_cloud.copilot) instead of as absolute. Also: FTS5 parses hyphenated terms (oauth2-proxy) as column references, causing SQL errors. GH#31.
design:
## Fuzzy Scope Resolution

### Problem
QualifyScope is a pure string function that blindly prepends ScopePrefix. Users typing --scope copilot from project the_cloud get the_cloud.copilot (doesn't exist) instead of copilot (exists).

### Algorithm: App.ResolveScope(ctx, userInput)
1. "" → DefaultScope (unchanged)
2. "/foo" → "foo" (literal escape hatch, unchanged)
3. Try qualified: prefix + "." + input → Scope.Get succeeds → use it
4. Try bare: input as-is → Scope.Get succeeds → use it
5. Suffix match: find scopes ending in "." + input (or equal to input)
   - Exactly one match → use it
   - Multiple matches → use qualified form, print warning listing collisions
6. Fall back to qualified (no scope exists yet — conservative default)

### Key design decisions
- Read-only: recall, search, list, show, stats, conflicts, export use ResolveScope
- Write-path (add, update) keeps QualifyScope for deterministic scope placement
- Ambiguity warning: when multiple suffix matches exist, warn user with the collision list so they can disambiguate with / prefix
- Method lives on App (needs DB access via Scopes repo), not AppConfig
- Implementation: list all scopes (small table), match in Go — one cheap query
- / escape hatch unchanged for power users

### Secondary fix: FTS5 hyphen parsing
Hyphenated terms like oauth2-proxy cause SQL errors in FTS5 queries. Need to quote/escape terms containing hyphens before passing to MATCH clause.
acceptance_criteria:
1. --scope copilot and --scope /copilot return identical results when copilot is unambiguous, regardless of CWD
2. Qualified scope (prefix.input) takes priority when it exists
3. Suffix match works: --scope auth finds foo.auth if it's the only scope ending in auth
4. Ambiguous suffix matches print a warning listing colliding scopes and fall back to qualified form
5. Write commands (add, update) unchanged — still use QualifyScope for deterministic placement
6. / prefix escape hatch works unchanged for explicit cross-project access
7. Hyphenated terms in FTS5 queries are properly quoted and do not cause SQL errors
8. Tests cover: qualified wins, bare fallback, unique suffix, ambiguous suffix with warning, / literal
notes:
## Implementation

### Fuzzy scope resolution (cmd/scope_resolve.go)
- New App.ResolveScope(ctx, input) method with 6-step fallback algorithm
- Replaces QualifyScope on 7 read commands: recall, search, list, show, stats, conflicts, export
- Write commands (add, update) unchanged — still use QualifyScope
- Ambiguity warning goes to os.Stderr to avoid polluting piped output
- Tests in cmd/scope_resolve_test.go with stubScopeRepo mock

### FTS5 hyphen fix (storage/sqlite/entry.go)
- sanitizeFTS5Query() quotes tokens containing FTS5 operator chars (-, :, +, ^, parens, brackets, ~)
- Preserves trailing * for prefix search (auth*)
- Preserves already-quoted tokens
- Applied in SearchText before MATCH clause
- Unit tests in storage/sqlite/fts_sanitize_test.go
close_reason: Implemented fuzzy scope resolution with progressive fallback (qualified → bare → suffix match) on all read commands. FTS5 hyphen parsing fixed with token quoting. Write commands unchanged for deterministic placement. Ambiguity warnings printed to stderr.

title: Investigate: edge creation UX for LLM agents
description: Creating edges (known link) requires full ULIDs which are only available via search or --json list. The recall command (primary LLM interface) hides IDs by design. This forces agents into a multi-step workflow: list as JSON -> parse -> copy ULIDs -> link. Error-prone (ULID typos) and context-expensive. Need to investigate better approaches: title-based linking, fuzzy ID matching, batch operations, or a higher-level 'relate' command.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Document specific friction points from dogfooding session\n2. Propose 2-3 concrete UX improvements\n3. Evaluate trade-offs for each approach
notes: Friction observed during dogfooding: (1) had to pipe --json list through python to get readable ID list, (2) ULID copy-paste error caused link failure, (3) recall output has no IDs so agent must switch to search to get linkable references, (4) each link is a separate command invocation
close_reason: Resolved by always including entry IDs in recall output (curly brace format). No flag required — IDs are cheap (26 chars) and always useful for agent workflows. Removed --ids flag in favor of always-on. Format: {ULID} at end of header line.

title: Generate architecture docs from bead history
description:
Beads capture architectural decisions, design rationale, and implementation context that should be preserved as committed documentation. Extract and organize this knowledge into docs/ so it's discoverable without needing to dig through issue history.

This involves:
1. Reviewing closed beads for architectural decisions, design notes, and rationale
2. Organizing into coherent docs (e.g. architecture overview, storage backend design, query engine design)
3. Committing to docs/ path in the repo
4. Deciding on format and structure (plain markdown, ADR format, etc.)
acceptance_criteria:
- A docs/ directory exists with architecture documentation derived from bead history
- Key design decisions and their rationale are captured (not just what, but why)
- Docs are committed to the repo and reviewable via normal git/GitHub browsing
- Coverage includes at least: high-level architecture, storage backend design, and any other major subsystems with significant bead history

title: Fix N+1 query in stats command
description: runStats in cmd/stats.go:34 loads ALL entries into memory then makes N+1 queries (EdgesFrom per entry) to count edges. For large knowledge graphs this is very expensive. Should use SQL COUNT queries instead.
acceptance_criteria: Stats command uses count queries, not full entry loading. Performance is O(1) not O(n).

title: Bug: --ttl 0 creates immediately-expired entries
description: When passing --ttl 0 to known add or known update, SetTTL(0) sets ExpiresAt = CreatedAt + 0 = CreatedAt, making the entry born-expired and invisible to search/recall. Expected: --ttl 0 should mean 'no expiry' (permanent), clearing both TTL and ExpiresAt fields.
acceptance_criteria:
1. known add 'test' --ttl 0 creates entry with no ExpiresAt
2. Entry is visible in recall and search
3. known update <id> --ttl 0 clears expiration on existing entry
close_reason: Fixed in add.go: --ttl 0 now checks for string '0' before calling SetTTL, matching the existing update.go behavior. Entry gets nil TTL and nil ExpiresAt (permanent). Added 6 unit tests in add_test.go.

comments.author: Dustin
comments.text: The bug is specifically in add.go:72-80. When --ttl 0 is passed, time.ParseDuration('0') returns 0, which flows to SetTTL(0), setting ExpiresAt=CreatedAt. The update command (update.go:91-94) already handles this correctly by checking for '0' string and clearing TTL/ExpiresAt. Fix: add the same '0' check in add.go before calling SetTTL.

title: SQLite as default storage backend
description:
Replace PostgreSQL as the default storage backend with SQLite for zero-config deployment. PostgreSQL is too heavyweight for an MVP CLI tool — requires a running server, pgvector extension install, and connection configuration. SQLite gives: single binary, no server, zero config, file-based storage portable across machines.

Key migrations needed:
- pgvector → sqlite-vec (via ncruces/go-sqlite3 WASM bindings for pure Go)
- ltree → recursive CTEs + LIKE queries with indexes
- JSONB + GIN → json_extract() + computed column indexes  
- TIMESTAMPTZ → TEXT (ISO 8601)
- Connection pooling → single connection with WAL mode + busy_timeout

PostgreSQL remains as an opt-in backend for users who need concurrent multi-process writes or large-scale (>100K entries) vector search with ANN indexing.

The existing storage interfaces (EntryRepo, EdgeRepo, ScopeRepo) are already database-agnostic — this is primarily an implementation task, not a refactor.
design:
## Research Summary (Feb 2026)

### Driver Selection
**Recommended: ncruces/go-sqlite3** (pure Go via wazero WASM runtime)
- No CGO required — preserves single-binary zero-config deployment
- Only pure-Go path to sqlite-vec integration (via WASM bindings)
- Performance: ~2x slower than mattn/go-sqlite3 for inserts, competitive for reads
- Alternative: mattn/go-sqlite3 if CGO is acceptable (fastest, full extension support)

### sqlite-vec for Vector Search
- Successor to sqlite-vss. Pure C, zero dependencies. By Alex Garcia (Mozilla).
- Go bindings: github.com/asg017/sqlite-vec-go-bindings/ncruces (WASM) or /cgo
- Brute-force KNN only (no ANN indexing like pgvector's IVFFlat/HNSW)
- Sufficient for <100K entries. Beyond that, PostgreSQL backend recommended.
- Supports: cosine, L2, inner product distance metrics. Float32 + int8 quantized vectors.

### Feature Migration Map
| PostgreSQL | SQLite | Complexity |
|---|---|---|
| pgvector vector type + <=> | sqlite-vec vec0 virtual tables | Medium |
| ltree hierarchy | LIKE 'parent.%' + indexes | Low |
| JSONB + GIN | json_extract() + computed col indexes | Low-Med |
| TIMESTAMPTZ | TEXT (ISO 8601) | Low |
| pgxpool connection pooling | Single conn + WAL mode | Low |
| ON CONFLICT + RETURNING | Supported (SQLite 3.35+) | Low |
| Partial index with NOW() | Regular index, query-time filter | Low |

### Concurrency Model
- WAL mode: readers never block writers, writers never block readers
- Single writer at a time (SQLITE_BUSY with retry on contention)
- PRAGMA busy_timeout=5000 for automatic retry
- PRAGMA foreign_keys=ON per connection (disabled by default in SQLite)
- Sufficient for single-user CLI. Multi-agent use needs busy_timeout.
acceptance_criteria: 1. 'known add "test"' works with zero config on a fresh install (no PostgreSQL, no env vars, no config files). 2. SQLite database auto-created at ~/.known/known.db. 3. All existing CLI commands work against SQLite backend. 4. PostgreSQL backend still works when DSN starts with postgres://. 5. 'known search' returns correct results using sqlite-vec vector similarity. 6. Scope hierarchy queries work via LIKE/recursive CTEs (no ltree dependency). 7. Existing integration tests pass against both backends (or have backend-specific variants). 8. Binary size increase from sqlite-vec WASM < 15MB.
notes:
**Key research findings (Feb 2026)**:

1. **sqlite-vec is the successor to sqlite-vss**. sqlite-vss (Faiss-based) is deprecated. sqlite-vec is pure C, zero deps, by Alex Garcia (Mozilla-sponsored). Stable v0.1.0+.

2. **FTS5 BM25 > PostgreSQL ts_rank**: SQLite FTS5's built-in bm25() implements proper TF-IDF. PostgreSQL's ts_rank only does TF (no corpus-wide IDF). Teams report PostgreSQL FTS degrading to 25-30s on 800K rows with ts_rank sorting. This is a genuine advantage for SQLite.

3. **ncruces/go-sqlite3 is the only pure-Go path to sqlite-vec**: modernc.org/sqlite (transpiled C→Go) cannot load C extensions. ncruces uses wazero (pure Go WASM runtime) and sqlite-vec provides WASM bindings specifically for it.

4. **SQLite recursive CTEs**: Fully supported since 3.8.3 (2014). Missing vs PostgreSQL: no CYCLE clause (manual cycle detection needed), no SEARCH DEPTH/BREADTH FIRST clause. Both irrelevant for known's scope hierarchy use case.

5. **PRAGMA gotchas**: foreign_keys OFF by default (must enable per connection), busy_timeout needed for concurrent access, journal_mode=WAL must be set explicitly.

6. **Scale ceiling**: sqlite-vec is brute-force KNN only (no ANN indexing). Practical for tens of thousands of entries. For >100K entries, PostgreSQL with pgvector HNSW indexes is the right backend.
close_reason: Epic complete: SQLite is now the default storage backend. Backend interface (known-74m), SQLite implementation (known-71c), and default DSN (known-n1o) all implemented. Zero-config story works: 'known add test' creates ~/.known/known.db automatically.

title: Add tests for embed/config.go LoadConfig and defaults
description: embed/config.go LoadConfig() and defaults() are at 0% coverage. These have no external dependencies and control which embedder is selected. The default embedder (hugot) selection logic is untested.
acceptance_criteria: Tests cover default config values, environment variable overrides, and validation of embedder types.

title: Decide and implement known init coexistence with plugin
description: With the plugin available, 'known init' still scaffolds into .claude/ for standalone use. Need to decide: (a) keep both paths, (b) have init detect if plugin is installed, (c) add --standalone flag. Current recommendation: keep init as-is for standalone users, document that plugin users should use 'claude plugin install' instead. Add a note to init output if it detects a plugin is already active.
acceptance_criteria:
1. 'known init' continues to work for standalone users
2. Documentation clarifies when to use init vs plugin install
3. No conflicts if both plugin and init scaffolding coexist
close_reason: Decision: keep 'known init' as-is for standalone users. Plugin and init serve different audiences — plugin for cross-project/team use, init for per-project standalone setup. Plugin README documents both paths. No code changes to known init needed.

title: AGENTS.md support in scaffold
description: known init currently only emits .claude/CLAUDE.md for project instructions. AGENTS.md is an open standard (Linux Foundation, backed by Google/OpenAI/Sourcegraph) used by 60k+ projects and supported by Codex, Cursor, OpenClaw, Copilot, and others. Emitting AGENTS.md at project root would make known init work for any AGENTS.md-compatible agent without framework-specific adapters.
acceptance_criteria:
- known init --agent generic (or similar flag) writes AGENTS.md at project root with known usage instructions
- AGENTS.md content is equivalent to current .claude/CLAUDE.md template (adapted for generic agent audience)
- Existing --no-scaffold behavior unchanged
- Default behavior (no --agent flag) continues to emit .claude/CLAUDE.md for backward compatibility

title: Add total limit and final sort to SearchHybrid
description: SearchHybrid returns all vector results plus all expanded results with no total limit. The vector phase is bounded by opts.Vector.Limit (hardcoded to 5 in recall), but expansion can add unbounded entries if the graph is densely connected. The --limit flag in cmd/recall.go:46 (default 20) is never applied to the final hybrid result set — it only works in scope-listing mode. Users passing --limit 3 still get 5 vector hits plus all their graph neighbors. Location: cmd/recall.go:92, query/hybrid.go:70
acceptance_criteria: HybridOptions has a TotalLimit field. Final result set is sorted by score and truncated to TotalLimit. The --limit flag in recall command controls the total output count. Tests verify truncation and sorting.

title: Add ConcurrentModificationError test to sqlite_test.go
description: ConcurrentModificationError and IsConcurrentModification() in storage/storage.go are at 0% coverage. The optimistic concurrency control path is never exercised in tests despite being a critical correctness mechanism. Need a test that performs a concurrent update conflict and asserts the error type.
acceptance_criteria: Test creates entry, reads it twice, updates via first read, then attempts update via second read (stale version). Asserts IsConcurrentModification(err) is true.

title: Batch SQL IN clause in loadLabelsForEntries
description: loadLabelsForEntries in storage/sqlite/entry.go:806 and storage/postgres/entry.go:783 build WHERE entry_id IN (?, ?, ...) with one placeholder per entry. SQLite has a default limit of 999 parameters (SQLITE_MAX_VARIABLE_NUMBER). An unfiltered List call with >999 entries will produce a SQL error.
acceptance_criteria: IN clause is batched into groups of 500 or fewer. Test covers batch boundary (e.g. 1000+ entries with labels).

title: SQLite storage implementation (storage/sqlite/)
description:
Implement EntryRepo, EdgeRepo, and ScopeRepo for SQLite behind the Backend interface.

Driver: ncruces/go-sqlite3 (pure Go via wazero WASM runtime). This avoids CGO and preserves the single-binary zero-config story.

Key implementation details:
- Vector search: sqlite-vec extension via WASM bindings (github.com/asg017/sqlite-vec-go-bindings/ncruces). Brute-force KNN, sufficient for <100K entries.
- Scope hierarchy: Replace ltree operators (@>, <@) with 'path LIKE parent.%' queries + indexes. Recursive CTEs for tree walks.
- JSON metadata: json_extract() for reads, TEXT storage. Computed column indexes for known query patterns (e.g., CREATE INDEX idx_meta_key ON entries(json_extract(meta, '$.key'))).
- Timestamps: ISO 8601 TEXT columns. Parse in Go.
- Concurrency: WAL mode enabled on connect. PRAGMA busy_timeout=5000. PRAGMA foreign_keys=ON per connection.
- Content hashing: Compute SHA-256 in Go (already done), store as TEXT.
- ON CONFLICT + RETURNING: Supported in SQLite 3.35+ (all modern drivers).
- Partial index for NOW(): Not possible with non-deterministic functions. Use regular index, filter at query time.
- Migrations: Embed SQL files, run at Open() time (same pattern as postgres/).

Schema will mirror PostgreSQL but adapted: no ltree type, no vector type (use vec0 virtual tables), TEXT for timestamps.
acceptance_criteria: 1. storage/sqlite/ package implements EntryRepo, EdgeRepo, ScopeRepo. 2. Vector similarity search works via sqlite-vec (cosine distance, top-K with threshold). 3. Scope hierarchy queries work: list children, subtree search, parent lookup. 4. JSONB metadata stored as TEXT, queryable via json_extract(). 5. Optimistic concurrency control (version column) works identically to PostgreSQL impl. 6. Content hash dedup (UNIQUE on content_hash+scope) works. 7. TTL expiration and GC work with TEXT timestamps. 8. Foreign keys enforced (PRAGMA foreign_keys=ON). 9. WAL mode enabled, busy_timeout=5000. 10. Migrations embedded and run at Open(). 11. Unit tests mirror postgres/ test coverage. 12. Pure Go build (CGO_ENABLED=0) succeeds.
close_reason: Full SQLite storage backend implemented with pure Go cosine/L2 distance (no sqlite-vec dependency). Uses ncruces/go-sqlite3 standard embed. 42 tests pass.

title: Unified Backend interface with factory pattern
description:
Extract a unified Backend interface from the existing storage layer to enable clean switching between SQLite and PostgreSQL at startup.

Current state: EntryRepo, EdgeRepo, ScopeRepo are separate interfaces in storage/storage.go. The postgres.DB struct exposes them but there's no common Backend interface.

Needed:
- Backend interface: Entries() EntryRepo, Edges() EdgeRepo, Scopes() ScopeRepo, WithTx(ctx, fn) error, Close() error, Migrate() error
- Factory function: NewBackend(dsn string) (Backend, error) that inspects the DSN to select postgres:// vs file: or sqlite:// 
- postgres.DB already satisfies this shape, just needs the interface extracted
- Update cmd/root.go to use the factory instead of directly calling postgres.Open()

This must land before the SQLite implementation so the interface contract is clear.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Backend interface defined in storage/storage.go with: Entries() EntryRepo, Edges() EdgeRepo, Scopes() ScopeRepo, WithTx(ctx, func(context.Context) error) error, Close() error, Migrate() error. 2. postgres.DB satisfies Backend interface (compile-time check via var _ Backend = (*DB)(nil)). 3. NewBackend(dsn string) factory function routes postgres:// to PostgreSQL, file paths / empty to SQLite. 4. cmd/root.go uses NewBackend() instead of direct postgres.Open(). 5. All existing tests pass without modification. 6. No SQLite implementation yet — just the interface and factory skeleton that returns an error for non-postgres DSNs until known-71c lands.
close_reason: Backend interface added to storage.go, postgres adapted to satisfy it, cmd/backend.go factory routes DSN to postgres or sqlite. All tests pass.

title: Google Gemini embedding adapter
description:
Add a Google Gemini-specific embedder implementation. Gemini's embedding API differs substantially from OpenAI:

- Endpoint: POST https://generativelanguage.googleapis.com/v1beta/models/{model}:embedContent
- Auth: API key as query param (?key=<key>) or OAuth Bearer token
- Request body: {content: {parts: [{text: '...'}]}, taskType: 'RETRIEVAL_DOCUMENT'|'RETRIEVAL_QUERY'|'SEMANTIC_SIMILARITY', title: '...' (optional)}
- Response: {embedding: {values: [...]}} (not the OpenAI [{embedding: [...]}] array format)
- Batch endpoint: :batchEmbedContents with different request shape
- Models: text-embedding-004 (768 dims), embedding-001 (768 dims)
- taskType parameter affects quality similar to Cohere's input_type

Config: KNOWN_EMBEDDER=gemini, KNOWN_EMBED_API_KEY=<key>, KNOWN_EMBED_MODEL=text-embedding-004
acceptance_criteria: 1. GeminiEmbedder implements Embedder interface. 2. Embed() sends taskType='RETRIEVAL_DOCUMENT'. 3. EmbedQuery() sends taskType='RETRIEVAL_QUERY'. 4. Auth via API key as query parameter (?key=...) or Authorization: Bearer for OAuth. 5. Endpoint: POST /v1beta/models/{model}:embedContent. 6. Batch via :batchEmbedContents endpoint. 7. Default model: text-embedding-004 (768 dims). 8. Parses Gemini response format: {embedding: {values: [...]}}. 9. Unit tests with mocked HTTP verify request/response format. 10. Error handling for quota limits and auth failures.

title: Wrap entry + link creation in a transaction
description: In cmd/add.go:139-175, the entry is persisted at line 139, then edges for --link flags are created one at a time in a loop. If the second of two --link flags fails (e.g., bad target ID), the entry and first edge are already committed, leaving the graph in an inconsistent state. The entry creation and all link creations should be wrapped in a single transaction via WithTx. Location: cmd/add.go:139-175
acceptance_criteria: Entry creation and all --link edge creations are wrapped in a single database transaction. If any link creation fails, the entire operation (including the entry) is rolled back. Tests verify atomicity — a failed link does not leave a partial entry+edge.

title: Approximate Nearest Neighbor (ANN) index for SQLite backend
description: The SQLite SearchSimilar implementation does a brute-force linear scan of all embeddings matching the dimension+scope. This is O(n) per query and will not scale beyond ~10K entries. Evaluate ANN approaches for sub-linear search: (1) HNSW (Hierarchical Navigable Small World) — the standard for high-recall ANN. Pure Go implementations exist (e.g. viterin/vek, or custom). (2) IVF (Inverted File Index) — partition vectors into clusters, search only nearby clusters. Simpler but lower recall. (3) sqlite-vec extension — a C extension providing virtual tables with KNN support, but adds CGO dependency. (4) Product Quantization — compress vectors for faster distance computation with approximate results. Recommendation: start with HNSW. It offers the best recall/speed tradeoff and is what pgvector uses internally. A pure Go implementation keeps the zero-CGO advantage of our SQLite backend.
acceptance_criteria: ANN index is integrated into SQLite SearchSimilar path. Benchmark shows sub-linear scaling: 10K entries query time is <10x the 1K entry time (brute force would be ~10x). Recall@10 >= 0.95 compared to exact search.

title: Add release workflow (GitHub Actions)
description: Add a GitHub Actions workflow that triggers on version tag push (v*). Runs goreleaser to build binaries and create a GitHub Release with changelog and artifacts.
acceptance_criteria:
1. .github/workflows/release.yml exists
2. Triggers on push of tags matching v*
3. Checks out code with fetch-depth: 0 (goreleaser needs full history)
4. Runs goreleaser release
5. Uses GITHUB_TOKEN for creating the release

title: Write plugin.json manifest
description: Create the .claude-plugin/plugin.json manifest file with correct schema. Must include name, version, description, author, repository, keywords, and pointers to skills/ and hooks/ directories.
acceptance_criteria:
1. plugin.json has name='known', version matching go module version
2. description, author, repository, keywords fields populated
3. skills and hooks fields point to correct relative paths
4. JSON validates against Claude Code plugin schema
close_reason: plugin.json manifest created with name, version, description, author, repository, license, homepage, keywords, and inline SessionStart hook. Follows beads plugin pattern — hooks defined inline in plugin.json rather than separate directory.

title: Document binary dependency and install prerequisites
description: The plugin shells out to the 'known' CLI binary. Users must have it installed. Add clear install instructions to the plugin README/CLAUDE.md. Include: go install command, link to GitHub releases (once available), and graceful error messages when binary is missing.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Plugin README documents installation prerequisites
2. Skills produce clear error if 'known' binary not found
3. SessionStart hook fails gracefully (already does via || true)
close_reason: Plugin README.md documents prerequisites (go install or GitHub Releases), installation methods (marketplace and local), and the plugin vs 'known init' distinction. SessionStart hook already has graceful fallback via || true.

title: Add HNSW index to Postgres
description: The Postgres backend uses pgvector distance operators but has no HNSW or IVFFlat index on the embedding column. Without an ANN index, pgvector falls back to exact brute-force scan — same O(n) complexity as the SQLite application-side scan but with network overhead. Additionally, the embedding column is declared as vector without a fixed dimension, which prevents ANN index creation (pgvector requires fixed dimensions for HNSW). This is the single highest-impact scaling improvement available. Location: storage/postgres/migrations/
acceptance_criteria: Migration adds HNSW index on the embedding column. Embedding dimension is pinned (e.g., vector(384)) or a strategy for multi-dimension support is implemented. Postgres search at 10K+ entries is measurably faster than without index. Documentation notes the dimension requirement.

title: Add weight decay to reinforcement
description: The reinforcement module (query/reinforce.go) can only increase edge weights, never decrease them. Over time, all frequently-used edges converge to the MaxWeight cap (1.0), making edge weight meaningless as a signal — the "saturation problem." Without decay, reinforcement becomes ineffective after enough sessions. Additionally, all action types (show, update, link, delete) get the same 0.05 boost despite different signal strengths. Location: query/reinforce.go
acceptance_criteria: Edge weights decay over time (e.g., multiply by decay factor per reinforcement cycle, or use time-decaying average). Differentiated boost amounts by action type (e.g., show: 0.02, update: 0.05, link: 0.05). Tests verify that unused edges decay below their initial weight over multiple cycles. Tests verify weight saturation is prevented.

title: Add release cycle
description:
Set up a release cycle for the known CLI tool. Currently there are no git tags, no CI, no release automation, and no changelog. Users install via 'go install ...@latest' which builds from source on HEAD. A release cycle would give users stable, versioned releases with pre-built binaries.

Key areas to address:
- Semantic versioning with git tags (start at v0.1.0 or v1.0.0)
- GitHub Actions CI (test on push/PR)
- Release automation (goreleaser for cross-platform binaries + GitHub Releases)
- Changelog (CHANGELOG.md or auto-generated from conventional commits)
- Version embedding in the binary (ldflags or go build info)
- Go module versioning (tags enable go install ...@v0.1.0)
design:
Decisions:
- Start at v0.1.0 (pre-stable)
- Use goreleaser for release automation (standard for Go CLIs)
- Auto-generate changelogs (goreleaser has built-in changelog generation from commits)
- Conventional commits recommended to get meaningful changelog categories

Implementation approach:
1. Embed version in binary first (foundation for everything else)
2. Add CI pipeline (tests must pass before releases matter)
3. Add goreleaser config with changelog generation
4. Create release workflow triggered by tag push
5. Tag v0.1.0 as first release
acceptance_criteria:
1. Git tags follow semver (vX.Y.Z)
2. CI runs tests on push to main and on PRs
3. Tagged pushes trigger automated release builds
4. GitHub Releases contain pre-built binaries for linux/darwin amd64/arm64
5. The binary reports its version (known --version or known version)
6. A CHANGELOG or release notes exist for each release

title: Prototype knowledge discovery agent
description: Create a Task-spawnable agent that walks a codebase and stores curated architectural knowledge via known. Not an AST indexer — focuses on high-signal summaries: entry points, conventions, architectural decisions, domain concepts mapped to code. Uses directory structure for scope hierarchy, prioritizes READMEs/configs/entry points/models, skips vendor/generated/test fixtures.
design: Approach: agent prompt (not new binary) that uses Glob, Read, Grep, and Bash (for known commands). Follows research best practices: selective extraction, file-tree awareness, multi-level summaries. Anti-patterns to avoid: over-extraction, embedding everything, ignoring structure.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Agent can be spawned via Task tool with a project root path
2. Creates scope hierarchy from directory structure
3. Stores atomic facts per significant directory with source-ref pointing to analyzed files
4. Produces quality comparable to manual dogfooding session entries
5. Test: run against services-runtime, compare output to manually curated entries
close_reason: Created /known:discover skill as plugin command (plugin/commands/discover.md). Tested against services-runtime: agent produced 21 entries, 13 edges, 4 new scopes with higher quality than manual indexing — captured implementation details like cache-line alignment, topological sort, ray-marched shadows that manual pass missed. Prompt structure: 5 phases (orientation, scope creation, directory walk, cross-cutting knowledge, summary) with quality standards and density targets.

title: Expand LLM embedding provider support
description:
The current embedder layer supports hugot (default), ollama, and openai-compatible. The openai-compatible backend works for providers that exactly match the OpenAI /v1/embeddings API shape, but several major providers deviate in meaningful ways:

- **Mistral**: Uses Bearer auth but different base URL conventions, and their batch endpoint has different size limits. The embedding models (mistral-embed) use a different response format for usage stats.
- **Cohere**: Completely different API shape. Requires 'input_type' parameter (search_document vs search_query) which affects embedding quality significantly. Different auth header (Authorization: Bearer vs X-API-Key in older versions).  
- **Google/Gemini**: Uses API key as query param or OAuth, different endpoint structure (/v1beta/models/MODEL:embedContent), different request/response shape, supports task_type parameter.
- **Voyage AI**: Specialized embedding provider. Different API shape, supports input_type parameter like Cohere.
- **Local alternatives**: vLLM and LiteLLM are OpenAI-compatible (already covered). Sentence-transformers via HTTP would need a custom adapter.

Approach: Add explicit adapters for providers that deviate from OpenAI's API. Keep openai-compatible as the catch-all for API-compatible providers. Each adapter handles auth, request format, response parsing, and provider-specific features (like input_type for asymmetric embeddings).
design:
## Research Summary (Feb 2026)

### Provider API Landscape

**OpenAI-compatible (already covered by existing adapter):**
- OpenAI, Azure OpenAI, vLLM, LiteLLM, together.ai, Groq, Anyscale
- POST /v1/embeddings, Bearer auth, {input: [...], model: '...'}
- Mistral is *mostly* compatible but verify batch limits

**Providers needing dedicated adapters:**

1. **Cohere** — Different API shape entirely. POST /v2/embed. Critical: requires 'input_type' param (search_document vs search_query) for asymmetric embeddings. Without it, retrieval quality degrades ~15-20%. Models: embed-english-v3.0 (1024d), embed-multilingual-v3.0 (1024d).

2. **Google Gemini** — Different auth (API key as query param or OAuth), different endpoint structure (/v1beta/models/MODEL:embedContent), different request/response JSON shape. Supports taskType param (RETRIEVAL_DOCUMENT/RETRIEVAL_QUERY). Models: text-embedding-004 (768d).

3. **Voyage AI** — Specialized embedding provider with asymmetric support. Different API shape.

**Providers without embedding APIs:**
- Anthropic (Claude) — No embedding API. Not relevant for this epic.

### Go SDK Landscape
- OpenAI: github.com/sashabaranov/go-openai (mature, well-maintained)
- Google: cloud.google.com/go/vertexai or REST API directly
- Cohere: github.com/cohere-ai/cohere-go (official)
- Mistral: No official Go SDK. REST API directly or openai-compatible.
- Most projects use direct HTTP clients rather than provider SDKs to avoid dependency bloat.

### Key Design Decision: Asymmetric Embeddings
Cohere, Gemini, and Voyage AI all support asymmetric embeddings where document and query vectors are generated differently. This materially improves retrieval quality. The Embedder interface needs an EmbedQuery() method (see known-9qz).
acceptance_criteria: 1. At least Cohere and Gemini adapters implemented alongside existing hugot/ollama/openai-compatible. 2. KNOWN_EMBEDDER config supports: hugot, ollama, openai-compatible, cohere, gemini. 3. Each adapter handles its provider's auth, request format, and response parsing. 4. Asymmetric embedding support available for providers that support it (Cohere, Gemini). 5. All adapters satisfy the Embedder interface (including EmbedQuery if known-9qz lands). 6. Unit tests with mocked HTTP for each adapter. 7. Integration tests (skippable) for each provider.
notes:
**Scope decision (Feb 2026)**: known's provider support is embeddings-only. Chat/completion APIs (including thinking block handling, streaming, tool_use responses) are out of scope — conflict resolution, summarization, and other LLM reasoning tasks are the caller's responsibility (e.g., the LLM agent using known as a tool). This keeps known focused: it stores and retrieves knowledge, it doesn't interpret it.

**Thinking blocks**: Provider differences in thinking/reasoning block handling (extended thinking in Claude, chain-of-thought in OpenAI o-series) are a chat completion concern, not an embedding concern. Not relevant to this epic.

**Provider classification for embeddings**:
- Truly OpenAI-compatible (no adapter needed): OpenAI, Azure OpenAI, vLLM, LiteLLM, together.ai, Groq, Anyscale, likely Mistral
- Need dedicated adapters: Cohere (input_type required), Google Gemini (different API shape + taskType), Voyage AI (input_type)
- No embedding API: Anthropic/Claude

**Asymmetric embedding impact**: Providers that support input_type/taskType report 15-20% retrieval quality improvement when document vs query intent is signaled. This is worth the interface change (known-9qz).

comments.author: Dustin
comments.text: DECISION: LLM provider support is embeddings-only. Chat/completion APIs (thinking blocks, streaming, tool_use, provider-specific response formats) are out of scope. known stores and retrieves knowledge — it doesn't interpret it. Conflict resolution, summarization, and reasoning are the caller's responsibility.

comments.author: Dustin
comments.text: NOTE: Scope confirmed as embeddings-only. The calling LLM is responsible for compacting findings into knowledge blocks before calling 'known add'. The compaction quality is the caller's problem — known just enforces the 4096-char content limit as a guardrail. No completion API surface needed. Provider differences in thinking blocks, streaming, tool_use responses are irrelevant to known's API.

title: Asymmetric embedding support (query vs document input_type)
description:
Several embedding providers (Cohere, Gemini, Voyage AI) produce better retrieval results when told whether the text being embedded is a document (for indexing) or a query (for searching). This is called 'asymmetric embedding' — the model produces slightly different vectors depending on the intended use.

Current state: The Embedder interface has Embed() and EmbedBatch() with no way to signal intent. All embeddings are produced identically regardless of whether they're for storage or query.

Options:
1. Add EmbedQuery(ctx, text) alongside Embed (used for storage). Embedders that don't support asymmetric mode just delegate to Embed().
2. Add a context key: embed.WithQueryMode(ctx) that embedders can check.
3. Add an InputType parameter to Embed/EmbedBatch.

Option 1 is cleanest — it's explicit, doesn't change the existing interface signature, and embedders that don't care just implement it as a wrapper.

Call sites:
- cmd/add.go, cmd/update.go → Embed() (document mode, for storage)
- query/vector.go → EmbedQuery() (query mode, for search)

This is a prerequisite for the Cohere and Gemini adapters to work optimally.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Embedder interface gains EmbedQuery(ctx, text) ([]float32, error) method. 2. Default implementation: EmbedQuery delegates to Embed (no behavior change for symmetric models). 3. HugotEmbedder.EmbedQuery delegates to Embed. 4. OllamaEmbedder.EmbedQuery delegates to Embed. 5. OpenAICompatibleEmbedder.EmbedQuery delegates to Embed. 6. CachedEmbedder wraps EmbedQuery with separate cache keys (query vs document). 7. query/vector.go calls EmbedQuery for search queries instead of Embed. 8. cmd/add.go and cmd/update.go continue using Embed (document mode). 9. All existing tests pass. 10. New test verifies EmbedQuery is called during search path.

title: AGENTS.md with LLM usage guidance for known
description: Write AGENTS.md that guides LLMs on how and when to use known as a knowledge persistence tool. Cover: what known is (persistent knowledge graph across sessions), when to store (decisions, patterns, conventions, debugging insights), when NOT to store (session-specific context, speculative conclusions, task progress), content discipline (atomic facts not essays, one idea per entry), scope conventions (project-derived, topic-based hierarchy), source-type guidance (agent vs manual vs documentation), recall vs search (recall for tool-use, search for exploration), the /remember /recall /forget /known-search skills, and .known.yaml project setup.
acceptance_criteria: 1. AGENTS.md contains guidance sections for: purpose, when to store, when not to store, content format, scope conventions, source types, skill usage. 2. Guidance is actionable — an LLM can follow it without ambiguity. 3. References the skills defined in known-l9k.
close_reason: LLM usage guidance implemented as embedded CLAUDE.md template in cmd/scaffold/templates/CLAUDE.md. Covers: purpose, skills reference, when to store/not store, content discipline, scopes, source types, confidence levels, TTL, recall vs search. Scaffolded to .claude/CLAUDE.md by 'known init'. Repo AGENTS.md left as-is for dev workflow.

title: Cross-scope guidance unclear on session init
description: When an agent starts a session and receives the scope tree, it can incorrectly assume it's in a scope it's not actually in. The scope tree output from the init hook should clearly indicate the agent's current auto-derived scope, or state that no existing scope matches. GH#22.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Init hook output shows the current auto-derived scope with a clear marker. 2. If the working directory doesn't match any scope, this is explicitly stated. 3. Agents no longer incorrectly assume they're in a different scope.
close_reason: Already fixed in ec51edd — scope tree shows 'you are here' marker for current auto-derived scope; explicitly states when no scope matches.

title: No warning when flags are silently dropped
description: When Go's flag.Parse stops at the first positional arg, remaining flags are treated as additional positional args and silently ignored. The CLI should either: (a) fix the parsing to support flags anywhere, or (b) at minimum warn when unconsumed args look like flags (start with -).
acceptance_criteria:
1. If a positional-only parse leaves unconsumed args starting with -, emit a warning
2. Or: switch to a flag library that supports interspersed flags (preferred solution)
close_reason: Duplicate of known-hc2 — fixing flag parsing to accept flags anywhere makes the warning unnecessary

title: Fix doc/code default mismatches
description: Multiple flag defaults in skill docs don't match code: threshold (0.3 vs 0.4), limit (10/20 vs 5), expand-depth (1 vs 0). These directly cause LLMs to construct wrong CLI invocations.
acceptance_criteria: All flag defaults in SKILL.md files and CLAUDE.md templates match config.go hardcoded defaults (threshold=0.4, limit=5, expand-depth=0, recency=0.1)
close_reason: Fixed all flag default mismatches in plugin command docs (recall, search): threshold 0.3→0.4, limit 10/20→5, expand-depth 1→0. Documented --text flag for FTS5 in both recall and search. All values now match config.go hardcoded defaults.

title: Evaluate daemon mode for embedding latency
description: Cold-start-to-first-result is 348ms with hugot (250ms model load + 98ms first embed). Acceptable for single-invocation CLI use (known add), but could compound in batch scenarios or tight loops. Investigate: (1) long-running daemon with model hot-loaded, CLI connects via unix socket, (2) persistent mmap of model weights, (3) smaller model (256-dim) to cut init time. Only pursue if users report latency as a pain point.
notes: Benchmarks from tu9.1 implementation: init=250ms, first embed=98ms, warm=69ms/embed. All on commodity hardware, CGO_ENABLED=0, all-MiniLM-L6-v2 384-dim.

title: Help flag exits with code 1 instead of 0
description: Running 'known --help' or 'known <command> --help' exits with status code 1. Standard CLI convention is exit 0 for help output. This is because the flag package returns an error for -help by default, and we propagate that error to os.Exit(1). Fix: detect flag.ErrHelp and exit 0.
acceptance_criteria: 1. 'known --help' exits 0. 2. 'known add --help' exits 0. 3. All other errors still exit 1.
close_reason: Detect pflag.ErrHelp in Run() error handler and return exit code 0 instead of 1

title: Fix SQLite scope hierarchy not created in runScopeCreate
description: runScopeCreate in cmd/scope.go:93 type-asserts to *postgres.ScopeStore to call EnsureHierarchy. On SQLite (the default backend), the assertion fails and the fallback only creates the leaf scope via Upsert, skipping parent hierarchy creation. E.g. 'known scope create project.auth.oauth' creates only the leaf, not 'project' and 'project.auth' ancestors.
acceptance_criteria: 'known scope create a.b.c' on SQLite creates scopes a, a.b, and a.b.c. Test covers SQLite path.

title: Fix scope tree hook output
description: Session hook output has three issues: 1) 'Knowledge available' is misleading when scopes exist but have no entries 2) Uses bare CLI form 'known recall' instead of skill naming convention 3) No self-describing framing for LLM context. Also uses 'known scope tree' in discover skill should use the same example form.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Hook output uses skill naming convention matching CLAUDE.md 2. Header is accurate (not 'Knowledge available' when graph may be empty) 3. Output has framing that helps LLMs identify what it is
close_reason: Changed scope tree header from misleading 'Knowledge available' to 'Scopes defined — use /recall to check for stored knowledge'. Changed example line to use skill naming convention (/recall) instead of bare CLI (known recall). Updated test expectations.

title: Streaming import: avoid reading entire file into memory
description: cmd/import.go:26 uses os.ReadFile to load entire import file into memory before processing. For JSONL, should stream directly from file with bufio.Scanner. For JSON array, should use json.NewDecoder on a reader. Current approach has unbounded memory usage.
acceptance_criteria: Import command streams from file without loading entire contents into memory. Works for both JSON and JSONL formats.

title: Rethink recall graph expansion vs related command overlap
description: recall with expand-depth=1 performs 1-hop graph expansion from each vector result. This means recall already surfaces most of what a follow-up 'related' call would find, making 'related' useless as an incremental discovery tool. Two possible fixes: (A) recall defaults to expand-depth=0 (pure vector, no graph expansion) and users explicitly use 'related' for graph traversal, or (B) recall's expansion is kept but 'related' is taught to exclude entries already seen in the previous recall. Either way, the two commands should have distinct, complementary roles.
acceptance_criteria: 1. recall and related have clearly distinct roles\n2. A typical workflow of 'recall then related' surfaces incrementally new information\n3. Default behavior doesn't require manual flag tuning to achieve this
close_reason: Changed recall default expand-depth from 1 to 0. Recall is now pure vector search by default — no graph expansion. The 'related' command retains its role as the graph traversal tool, giving it a distinct, incremental purpose after recall.

title: Add tests for pure functions: formatAge, parseDayDuration, FreshnessLabel
description: formatAge (cmd/list.go) has 7 untested branches (singular/plural for minutes, hours, days, plus default). parseDayDuration handles '7d'/'30d' suffix format. FreshnessLabel (model/types.go:171) formats Fresh/Aging/Stale/Expired. All are pure functions ideal for table-driven tests with zero dependencies.
acceptance_criteria: Table-driven tests cover all branches of formatAge, parseDayDuration, and FreshnessLabel.

title: ID completion for CLI
description: Add shell completion for entry IDs in the known CLI. Stretch goal: ergonomics to start from an idea (e.g. recall query) and manage the result concretely as though working by ID. GH#24.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Tab completion works for entry IDs in bash/zsh/fish. 2. Partial ID prefixes resolve unambiguously. 3. Completion integrates with existing shell completion framework.
close_reason: Already fixed across e34b0e0 (shell completion) and ec51edd (content-based entry resolution — commands accept queries, not just IDs, with semantic search fallback).

title: Update to non-existent scope gives raw SQLite FK error
description: Running 'known update <id> --scope new.scope' where 'new.scope' doesn't exist yet produces: 'error: update entry: update entry: sqlite3: constraint failed: FOREIGN KEY constraint failed'. Should either auto-create the scope (like add does) or give a clear error message suggesting 'known scope create new.scope' first.
acceptance_criteria:
1. 'known update <id> --scope nonexistent' either auto-creates the scope or returns a helpful error message mentioning scope creation
2. No raw SQLite errors exposed to the user
close_reason: Added EnsureHierarchy call to Update methods in both SQLite and Postgres storage layers, matching Create/CreateOrUpdate behavior

title: Session tracking for CLI usage correlation
description: Add session state to known.db so CLI invocations can be correlated across a single agent session. This is the missing primitive needed for signal-based edge reinforcement. Sessions are stored in known.db with a sessions table (id, started_at, scope, agent) and session_events table (id, session_id, timestamp, event_type, entry_ids, query, meta). The plugin sets a session ID at session start and passes it on all invocations. Every command that touches entries logs an event.
design:
## Key Design Constraint
Session tracking MUST be fully automatic. No flags, no extra commands, no LLM cooperation required. The user and agent should not know sessions exist — they just use known normally and session data accumulates silently.

## How It Works
- Plugin session-start hook creates a session and writes the ID to a known location (e.g., env var KNOWN_SESSION, or a file in .known/ or XDG_RUNTIME_DIR)
- Every CLI invocation checks for an active session and logs its event automatically
- Session expires after inactivity timeout (e.g., 30 min no commands)
- No --session flag needed — session discovery is automatic
acceptance_criteria: 1. sessions and session_events tables exist in schema (SQLite + Postgres migrations). 2. SessionRepo interface with Create/AddEvent/ListEvents/ActiveSession methods. 3. Session ID is managed automatically — created on first command invocation when none is active, reused for subsequent commands within the same process group or plugin session. No --session flag required from user or LLM. 4. Every entry-touching command (recall, search, show, add, update, link, delete) logs a session event transparently with zero user-visible output and zero extra flags. 5. Plugin session-start hook creates the session; all skill invocations inherit it automatically. 6. Tests cover session lifecycle and event logging.
close_reason: Merged PR #18: Session model, SessionRepo interface, SQLite/PostgreSQL implementations, session start/end commands, automatic event logging, plugin hook

title: Cohere embedding adapter
description:
Add a Cohere-specific embedder implementation. Cohere's API differs from OpenAI in important ways:

- Endpoint: POST https://api.cohere.com/v2/embed  
- Auth: Authorization: Bearer <key>
- Required 'input_type' parameter: 'search_document' for indexing, 'search_query' for queries. This is critical — using the wrong input_type significantly degrades retrieval quality for asymmetric embedding models.
- Request body: {texts: [...], model: 'embed-english-v3.0', input_type: '...', embedding_types: ['float']}
- Response shape differs from OpenAI
- Models: embed-english-v3.0 (1024 dims), embed-multilingual-v3.0 (1024 dims), embed-english-light-v3.0 (384 dims)

The Embedder interface may need a context hint for embed-time vs query-time to properly set input_type. Consider adding an optional EmbedForQuery method or a context key.

Config: KNOWN_EMBEDDER=cohere, KNOWN_EMBED_API_KEY=<key>, KNOWN_EMBED_MODEL=embed-english-v3.0
acceptance_criteria: 1. CohereEmbedder implements Embedder interface. 2. Embed() sends input_type='search_document' to Cohere API. 3. EmbedQuery() sends input_type='search_query'. 4. Auth via Authorization: Bearer header. 5. Batch support via EmbedBatch() with Cohere's batch limits respected. 6. Default model: embed-english-v3.0 (1024 dims). 7. Configurable: KNOWN_EMBED_MODEL, KNOWN_EMBED_API_KEY. 8. Unit tests with mocked HTTP verify request format including input_type. 9. Error handling for rate limits (429) and auth failures (401).

title: Fix shallow Metadata.Clone() — nested values share references
description: Metadata.Clone() in model/types.go:108 does a shallow copy of map[string]any. Nested maps or slices (common after JSON unmarshal) share references between original and clone. Edge.Reverse() uses Clone() believing it gets an independent copy, but mutations through the clone affect the original.
acceptance_criteria: Metadata.Clone() produces a deep copy. Test: clone, mutate nested value in clone, verify original is unchanged.

title: HTTP server mode (known serve)
description: Add a known serve command that runs a local REST API, making known language-agnostic for agent runtimes that cannot easily shell out. Python agents, TypeScript agents, and custom runtimes could integrate via HTTP instead of subprocess calls. The existing --json flag on all CLI commands shows machine-readable output is already a design consideration. Related to known-abe (daemon mode evaluation for embedding latency).
acceptance_criteria:
- known serve starts a local HTTP server (configurable port, default localhost:7100 or similar)
- REST endpoints for core operations: add, recall/search, show, list, delete, link/unlink
- JSON request/response format consistent with existing --json CLI output
- Graceful shutdown on SIGINT/SIGTERM
- Health check endpoint
- Optional API key/token for local auth

title: Limit HTTP response body size in embedder implementations
description: Both embed/ollama.go:109 and embed/openai.go:126 use io.ReadAll(resp.Body) with no size limit. A malicious or misconfigured embedding server could return an arbitrarily large response, causing OOM. KNOWN_EMBED_URL is user-configurable, making this a real attack surface.
acceptance_criteria: Both implementations use io.LimitReader to cap response body (e.g. 50MB). Test covers the limit being enforced.

title: Filter and limit recall
description: Expose filtering and limiting options that already exist in the search infrastructure through the recall command. The search command has --threshold, --recency, --hybrid, and --expand-depth flags. The storage layer supports source type, provenance level, staleness, and expiration filtering. None of these are available on recall. GitHub issue #13.
design:
## Implementation Plan

### Approach: Surface existing infrastructure as recall flags

The recall command (cmd/recall.go) hardcodes HybridOptions. The search command (cmd/search.go) already exposes most of these. The storage layer (storage/storage.go EntryFilter) has additional filtering not exposed anywhere.

### Changes Required

**cmd/recall.go** — Add flags:
- --threshold (float, default 0.3) → VectorOptions.Threshold
- --recency (float, default 0.1) → VectorOptions.RecencyWeight  
- --expand-depth (int, default 1) → HybridOptions.ExpandDepth
- --provenance (string: verified|inferred|uncertain) → EntryFilter.ProvenanceLevel
- --source (string: file|url|conversation|manual) → EntryFilter.SourceType
