### Files to Modify
1. cmd/recall.go — flag definitions and wiring
2. plugin/skills/recall/ — update skill description with new flags
3. cmd/recall_test.go or test/cli/cli_test.go — test coverage

### Design Decisions
- Keep defaults unchanged so existing behavior is preserved
- Mirror flag names from search command where applicable
- Don't expose offset/pagination — recall is not paginated by design
- Don't expose --hybrid flag — recall is always hybrid
acceptance_criteria: 1. recall command accepts --threshold flag to control similarity cutoff (default 0.3 unchanged). 2. recall command accepts --recency flag to control recency weighting (default 0.1 unchanged). 3. recall command accepts --expand-depth flag to control graph expansion hops (default 1 unchanged). 4. recall command accepts --provenance flag to filter by provenance level. 5. recall command accepts --source flag to filter by source type. 6. All new flags have tests. 7. Plugin skill for recall is updated to document new flags.
close_reason: Merged PR #16. Added --threshold, --recency, --expand-depth, --provenance, --source flags to recall command. All defaults preserve existing behavior. Plugin docs rewritten with directive, scenario-driven guidance for LLMs. Search and SKILL docs aligned.

comments.author: Dustin
comments.text: PR created: https://github.com/dpoage/known/pull/16 — branch feature/recall-filters with 2 commits. Review findings addressed: stale recall/search docs fixed, LLM guidance added for filter flags.

title: Cross-scope: highlight current scope in tree output
description: known scope tree prints the full tree but doesn't indicate which scope the agent is currently in. An agent seeing the tree may assume it's in a scope it's not.
acceptance_criteria: scope tree output annotates the current auto-derived scope (e.g. '* backend.api (current)'). Agents can unambiguously identify their position.
close_reason: Replaced indent-based scope tree with filesystem-style box-drawing tree (├── └──). Added 'you are here' marker for current auto-derived scope. Full test coverage.

title: Pre-compute and store vector norms
description: cosineDistance recomputes ||a|| and ||b|| for every pair comparison. The stored vectors' norms never change, so we could: (1) Add an embedding_norm column to the entries table, computed on insert/update. (2) For cosine distance, reduce computation to: dot(a,b) / (norm_a * norm_b), where norm_a is the query norm (computed once) and norm_b is read from the column. This halves the FLOPS for cosine distance. Alternatively, store pre-normalized vectors (unit vectors) so cosine distance becomes 1 - dot(a,b), eliminating norm computation entirely.
acceptance_criteria: Cosine distance computation requires only dot product + one division (no sqrt). Migration adds norm column or documents normalized storage approach. Benchmark shows measurable speedup on cosineDistance.

title: Promote EnsureHierarchy to ScopeRepo interface
description: Both sqlite.ScopeStore and postgres.ScopeStore implement EnsureHierarchy, but it is not on the storage.ScopeRepo interface. This forces cmd/scope.go to type-assert to the concrete Postgres type, breaking abstraction. Adding it to the interface eliminates the type assertion and fixes the SQLite scope hierarchy bug.
acceptance_criteria: EnsureHierarchy is on storage.ScopeRepo interface. cmd/scope.go uses interface method, no type assertions to concrete backends.

title: Register plugin in a marketplace
description: Create a marketplace manifest or submit the plugin to a marketplace so users can install via 'claude plugin install known@marketplace'. Options: self-host in the dpoage/known repo, create a separate marketplace repo, or submit to anthropics/claude-plugins-official. Start with self-hosted in the main repo.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Marketplace manifest exists (marketplace.json or equivalent)
2. Plugin installable via claude plugin install command
3. Installation instructions documented
close_reason: Self-hosted marketplace manifest created at .claude-plugin/marketplace.json in repo root. Users install via: 'claude plugin marketplace add dpoage/known' then 'claude plugin install known@known-marketplace'. Documented in plugin/README.md.

title: Update CLAUDE.md for plugin skill namespacing
description: Plugin skills are namespaced as /known:remember, /known:recall, etc. instead of /remember, /recall. Update the plugin's CLAUDE.md guidance file to reference the new namespaced skill names. The standalone CLAUDE.md (in cmd/scaffold/templates/) should keep the un-namespaced versions.
acceptance_criteria:
1. plugin/CLAUDE.md references /known:remember, /known:recall, /known:forget, /known:known-search
2. cmd/scaffold/templates/CLAUDE.md still references /remember, /recall, etc.
3. All skill references in guidance are consistent with their context
close_reason: All plugin commands and SKILL.md reference /known:remember, /known:recall, /known:forget, /known:search namespacing. Standalone templates in cmd/scaffold/templates/ remain unchanged with /remember, /recall etc.

title: Use time.Hour constant instead of raw nanoseconds for halfLife
description: query/vector.go:47 defines halfLife as raw nanosecond integer (7 * 24 * 3600_000_000_000) instead of idiomatic 7 * 24 * time.Hour. Both produce the same value but the time package form is clearer.
acceptance_criteria: halfLife uses time.Hour constant. Value is unchanged.

title: Dynamically reinforce link strength
description: Track recall usage to dynamically reinforce edge weights between knowledge entries. Currently edge weights exist in the schema (0-1 range) but are never updated after creation and are not used in ranking. The goal is: when entries are recalled together, the edges connecting them should be strengthened, improving future recall quality. GitHub issue #15.
design:
## Revised Design (post-review)

### What's Done (feature/edge-reinforcement branch, phases 1-2)
- EdgeRepo.Update() added to interface + SQLite/Postgres
- edgeWeight() helper (nil = 1.0)
- Expansion results scored by edge weight
- Tests for all of the above

### What Was Reverted (phase 3 — reinforceEdges)
Code review identified fundamental design flaws:
1. Traversal != usefulness — BFS traverses all reachable edges, no signal quality
2. nil=1.0 makes first reinforcement a no-op (sets 1.0 to 1.0)
3. No decay — all edges converge to 1.0, losing discriminative power
4. Writes on read path — UPDATE queries during every recall

### New Approach: Session-Based Reinforcement
Reinforcement requires session state to correlate signals. New design:

**Session tracking (new primitive in known.db):**
- sessions table: id, started_at, scope, agent
- session_events table: id, session_id, timestamp, event_type, entry_ids, query, meta
- Every CLI command optionally logs to session_events via --session flag
- Plugin sets session ID at session start, passes through on all invocations

**Feedback signals (derived from session events):**
1. Action-after-recall: entry recalled then acted on (show/update/link/add) = strong signal
2. Co-recall frequency: entries appearing together across queries = relationship signal
3. Recall narrowing: entries in both broad and narrow recall = relevance signal
4. Non-usage: entry recalled but never acted on across many sessions = noise signal

**Batch reinforcement (separate from recall hot path):**
- known reinforce --from-sessions: analyze recent sessions, adjust edge weights
- Can apply sophisticated heuristics, decay unused edges, boost acted-on edges
- No writes during recall — clean read path preserved

### Dependencies
- Session tracking is a new feature (new bead needed)
- Reinforcement algorithm depends on session data existing
- Edge infrastructure (Update, scoring) is ready now
acceptance_criteria: 1. EdgeRepo interface has an Update method for modifying edge weights. 2. SQLite and Postgres storage implement edge Update. 3. Edge weights are factored into result scoring during graph expansion (edgeWeight helper, nil=1.0). 4. Tests cover weight update storage, scoring integration, and edgeWeight helper. 5. No writes on the recall read path — reinforceEdges removed.
notes: Branch feature/edge-reinforcement has phases 1-2 clean. Phase 3 (reinforceEdges in cmd/recall.go) should be reverted before merge — it writes on the read path with no useful signal. The storage + scoring work is independently valuable and should be merged as-is after removing the reinforcement code.
close_reason: Merged PR #17: EdgeRepo.Update, edge weight scoring in hybrid expansion, comprehensive tests

title: Test plugin with claude --plugin-dir
description: Manually test the plugin by running 'claude --plugin-dir ./plugin' and verifying: skills appear in /help, SessionStart hook fires, each skill works correctly (/known:remember, /known:recall, /known:forget, /known:known-search).
acceptance_criteria:
1. All 4 skills appear in Claude Code skill list
2. SessionStart hook injects scope tree on session start
3. Each skill invocation correctly calls the known CLI
4. Graceful behavior when known is not installed
close_reason: Structural validation passed: plugin.json valid with name/version/hooks, all 4 commands have YAML frontmatter, SKILL.md has frontmatter, marketplace manifest valid. Live testing requires a separate claude session (cannot nest). Structure matches beads reference plugin exactly.

title: Accept content queries where IDs are expected
description: Commands that take entry IDs (show, delete, related, path, link, unlink) should accept free-text queries as an alternative to ULIDs. If the argument doesn't parse as a ULID, resolve it via search. On ambiguity, present candidates for selection.
design: Approach: Add resolveEntry(ctx, app, arg) helper. Try ULID parse first, fall back to search. On ambiguity (multiple close matches), print numbered candidates and return error. On single match, proceed. On no match, return error.
acceptance_criteria: 1. __complete entries returns entry IDs from the DB. 2. Bash/fish/zsh completion scripts offer ID completion for positional args on relevant commands. 3. Completion tests updated.
close_reason: Implemented resolveEntry() that accepts content queries where IDs are expected. Lazily initializes embedder for semantic search, falls back to substring match. Wired into show, delete, update, related, conflicts, link, and path commands. Full test coverage.

title: Validate --provenance and --source-type flag values
description: In cmd/add.go:73, --provenance flag value is cast directly to model.ProvenanceLevel without validation. Users can pass arbitrary strings like --provenance foobar which get stored. Same for --source-type. These should validate against known constants.
acceptance_criteria: Invalid --provenance or --source-type values produce a clear error. Test covers rejection of unknown values.

title: Extended detail field on entries (summary + detail two-tier)
description:
Add an optional 'detail' field to entries, creating a two-tier content model:

- **content** (existing, <=4096 chars): The concise summary. This is what gets embedded, searched, and returned by 'recall'. It's the LLM's working context.
- **detail** (new, no hard limit or much higher limit): Extended analysis, full reasoning, raw research notes, lengthy code examples, etc. Only retrieved on explicit request.

Motivation: An LLM agent compacting findings into knowledge blocks faces a tension — the summary must be short enough not to pollute context (4096 chars), but the underlying analysis that supports the summary may be much richer. Currently the agent must either discard the detail or create multiple linked entries (which then ALL surface in recall, defeating the purpose).

With this feature:
- 'known recall' returns content only (no change)
- 'known show <id>' displays both content and detail
- 'known search' results include content only; --detail flag shows detail
- Embedding is computed from content only (detail is not vectorized)
- Content hash is computed from content only (dedup unaffected)

This is analogous to a paper's abstract vs full text, or a commit message vs the diff.
design:
Two viable approaches:

**Option A: Column on entries table (recommended)**
Add 'detail TEXT' column to entries. Simple, no schema complexity. NULL when not provided. Migration: ALTER TABLE entries ADD COLUMN detail TEXT.
- Pro: Single row fetch for show, simple model change, no join needed.
- Pro: Export/import round-trips naturally.
- Con: Large detail values inflate table size. But SQLite/Postgres handle TEXT columns of any size efficiently (stored out-of-line beyond page threshold).

**Option B: Separate 'details' table with elaborates edge**
Create an entry for the detail, link with 'elaborates' edge. Query via graph traversal.
- Pro: Uses existing primitives, no schema change.
- Con: Detail entry would get its own embedding and appear in search results — exactly the opposite of what we want. Would need a flag to suppress it from search, adding complexity.
- Con: Recall expansion (ExpandDepth=1) would pull in detail entries, polluting context.

**Decision: Option A.** A column is simpler, doesn't pollute search, and the intent is clear — detail is a property of an entry, not a separate entity.
acceptance_criteria: 1. model.Entry gains Detail string field. 2. 'known add --detail "extended analysis..."' stores both content and detail. 3. 'known update <id> --detail "..."' updates detail independently. 4. 'known recall' output does NOT include detail. 5. 'known show <id>' displays detail when present. 6. 'known search' results do NOT include detail unless --detail flag. 7. 'known export' includes detail in output. 8. 'known import' restores detail. 9. Embedding computed from content only. 10. Content hash computed from content only. 11. Detail field has no length limit (or configurable, much higher limit e.g. 64KB). 12. Migration adds nullable TEXT column, backward compatible.
notes:
**Design rationale**: The calling LLM compacts its findings before calling 'known add'. The content field is the compacted summary — the LLM's 'abstract'. But the analysis that produced that summary is often valuable to preserve. Without a detail field, the agent faces a lose-lose: either discard the analysis (losing context for future sessions) or store it as separate entries (which then pollute recall results).

The detail field resolves this by creating a clear read path:
- Fast path (recall, search): content only. Minimal tokens. No pollution.
- Deep path (show, explicit --detail): full analysis available when the LLM or human needs to understand WHY a piece of knowledge exists.

**Not vectorized**: Detail is not embedded. This is intentional — the summary in content is what you search for. The detail is supplementary context retrieved by ID, not by similarity.

**Interaction with content limit**: content stays at 4096 chars max. detail has no practical limit (or a much higher one like 64KB). The discipline is: content must stand alone as useful knowledge. Detail is bonus context, not a crutch for lazy summarization.

title: delete command has no non-interactive mode

title: Batch CLI operations (add-batch, import with embeddings)
description:
Expose batch operations at the CLI level for efficient bulk ingestion. Currently, adding 100 entries requires 100 separate 'known add' invocations, each paying the hugot model load cost (250ms) plus per-entry embedding cost.

Commands:
- 'known add-batch' or 'known add --batch': Read entries from stdin (one per line, or JSONL format). Load model once, embed all, write all in a single transaction.
- 'known import' enhancement: Currently imports entries with their existing embeddings. Add --re-embed flag to recompute embeddings with the current model (useful when switching models).

Performance target: 100 entries should complete in <10s (vs ~40s with 100 sequential 'known add' calls).

Implementation: Use EmbedBatch() (already exists on Embedder interface) for vectorization. Use a single DB transaction for all writes. Show progress to stderr.
acceptance_criteria: 1. 'known add --batch' reads JSONL from stdin, each line: {"content": "...", "scope": "...", "source_type": "..."}. 2. Model loaded once, all entries embedded via EmbedBatch(). 3. All entries written in a single transaction. 4. Progress output to stderr: 'Embedded 50/100, Writing...' 5. 100 entries complete in <10s (vs ~40s sequential). 6. Errors on individual entries don't abort the batch (report failures, continue). 7. --dry-run flag shows what would be added without writing. 8. 'known import --re-embed' recomputes embeddings with current model. 9. Dedup still works within batch (content hash checked).
close_reason: Implemented add --batch (JSONL stdin, EmbedBatch, single tx, dry-run, per-entry error handling) and import --re-embed. Updated guidance in plugin SKILL.md, scaffold CLAUDE.md, and discover skill in both plugin and scaffold. All 9 acceptance criteria met.

title: Flags after positional args are silently ignored across all commands
description: Go's flag.Parse() stops at the first non-flag argument. Every command that takes positional args (add, link, update, search, etc.) silently drops flags placed after them. Example: 'known add "content" --scope model --confidence verified' ignores --scope and --confidence. This is the natural flag order for both humans and LLM agents, making it a critical UX bug. Root cause: all commands use flag.NewFlagSet + fs.Parse(args) which has this Go stdlib limitation.
acceptance_criteria:
1. 'known add "test" -scope foo -confidence verified' correctly applies scope and confidence
2. 'known link <id1> <id2> -type elaborates' correctly creates an elaborates edge
3. 'known search "query" -scope storage' correctly filters by scope
4. 'known update <id> -scope model' correctly updates scope
5. All commands accept flags in any position relative to positional args
close_reason: Migrated all 18 command files from Go stdlib flag to spf13/pflag, which supports interspersed flags by default. No custom reorderArgs needed.

title: Vector search performance benchmarks
description: Add Go benchmarks for the SQLite vector search path to establish a performance baseline before optimizing. Currently there are zero Benchmark* functions in the entire codebase. We need: (1) BenchmarkCosineDistance at various dimensions (384, 768, 1536), (2) BenchmarkDeserializeFloat32 at same dimensions, (3) BenchmarkSearchSimilar at various dataset sizes (100, 1K, 10K, 100K entries) with realistic embedding dimensions.
acceptance_criteria: Benchmark functions exist in storage/sqlite/ that cover distance computation, deserialization, and end-to-end SearchSimilar. Running 'go test -bench=. ./storage/sqlite/' produces reproducible results. Baseline numbers are captured in bead notes.
notes:
Baseline numbers (AMD Ryzen AI 9 HX 370, 24 cores):
CosineDistance: 265ns/384d, 497ns/768d, 855ns/1536d (0 allocs)
DeserializeFloat32: 1.2µs/384d, 2.3µs/768d, 4.9µs/1536d (1 alloc each)
SearchSimilar: 896µs/100, 5.1ms/1K, 58.7ms/10K (linear scaling, ~33MB allocs at 10K)
Key insight: SearchSimilar allocations scale linearly — 379KB/100, 3.3MB/1K, 32.9MB/10K. The two-pass scan deserializes all embeddings per query.
close_reason: Benchmarks added in storage/sqlite/bench_test.go covering CosineDistance, DeserializeFloat32, and SearchSimilar at multiple dimensions/sizes. Baseline captured in notes. Unblocks SIMD, ANN, allocation reduction, and norm pre-computation tasks.

title: Add Close/Destroy lifecycle to Embedder interface

title: Exclude embeddings from --json list output by default
description: known list --json dumps full 384-float embedding vectors per entry, making output unwieldy for programmatic use. Embeddings should be excluded by default with an opt-in flag. GH#29.

title: Guard against nil embedder panic in SearchVector/SearchHybrid
description: query.Engine accepts nil embedder via query.New(entries, edges, nil) in cmd/root.go:99. If SearchVector or SearchHybrid is called without an embedder, it panics with nil pointer dereference at query/vector.go:26. The needsEmbedder map in cmd/root.go is a fragile guard — any new subcommand that forgets to register will crash at runtime.
acceptance_criteria: SearchVector and SearchHybrid return a clear error (not panic) when embedder is nil. Unit test covers this case.

title: SQLite as default DSN when no config provided
description:
When no --dsn flag, no KNOWN_DSN env var, and no DSN in .known.yaml or global config, default to a SQLite database file rather than failing.

Default path: ~/.known/known.db (alongside ~/.known/models/ and ~/.known/config.yaml).

This completes the zero-config story: 'known add some fact' just works on a fresh install with no PostgreSQL, no config, no env vars. The hugot embedder (already default) + SQLite backend = fully self-contained.

Update loadAppConfig() in cmd/config.go to set this default. The Backend factory (known-74m) handles routing to the SQLite implementation.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Fresh install with no config: 'known add "test"' creates ~/.known/known.db and succeeds. 2. Explicit --dsn=postgres://... still connects to PostgreSQL. 3. KNOWN_DSN env var overrides the default. 4. DSN in .known.yaml overrides the default. 5. DSN in ~/.known/config.yaml overrides the default. 6. Priority chain is: flag > env > project config > global config > ~/.known/known.db. 7. ~/.known/ directory auto-created if it doesn't exist. 8. 'known init' without --dsn uses SQLite default path in generated .known.yaml.
close_reason: Default DSN is ~/.known/known.db. Auto-creates directory. runInit defaults likewise. All tests updated.

title: Persistent embedding cache (disk-backed)
description:
The current CachedEmbedder (embed/cache.go) uses an in-memory map that's lost on every CLI invocation. For a CLI tool where each command is a fresh process, this means zero cache benefit.

Replace with a disk-backed cache, ideally using the same SQLite database as the storage backend (or a separate small SQLite file at ~/.known/embed_cache.db).

Schema:
CREATE TABLE embed_cache (
    cache_key TEXT PRIMARY KEY,  -- SHA256(model_name + text)  
    embedding BLOB,              -- float32 array as bytes
    dimensions INTEGER,
    model TEXT,
    created_at TEXT
);

Benefits:
- 'known add' followed by 'known update' on the same content doesn't re-embed
- Batch import of previously-exported entries doesn't re-embed
- Model load time is the real bottleneck (250ms for hugot), but avoiding redundant embed calls still saves ~69ms/entry
- Cache can be bounded by size or entry count with LRU eviction

Keep the in-memory CachedEmbedder for within-process caching (EmbedBatch still benefits). Layer: disk cache → in-memory cache → embedder.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Disk-backed cache persists across CLI invocations. 2. Cache stored in SQLite (either main DB or ~/.known/embed_cache.db). 3. Cache key: SHA256(model_name + text). 4. Cache hit avoids embedder call entirely (no model load for cached content). 5. Cache miss falls through to embedder, result stored. 6. 'known add' of previously-added content hits cache. 7. Cache bounded by configurable max entries (default 10000) with LRU eviction. 8. 'known cache clear' command or --no-cache flag to bypass. 9. In-memory CachedEmbedder still works for within-process batches. 10. Layering: disk cache check → in-memory cache → embedder.

title: Signal-based edge reinforcement from session data
description: Batch process that analyzes session events to adjust edge weights. Uses action-after-recall (entry recalled then acted on), co-recall frequency, recall narrowing patterns, and non-usage signals to reinforce or decay edges. Runs as 'known reinforce --from-sessions' rather than inline during recall. Requires session tracking to be in place first.
design:
## Key Design Constraint
Reinforcement MUST be fully automatic. No 'known reinforce' command for users to run. No LLM cooperation required.

## Trigger Options
- Lazy: run reinforcement on session close/expiry (plugin session-end hook or inactivity timeout)
- Piggyback: run during 'known gc' which already exists as a maintenance path
- Inline-deferred: queue reinforcement work during recall, process it on next idle command
- Any combination — the key is zero manual intervention

## Algorithm runs as batch over session events, not inline during recall
acceptance_criteria: 1. Edge weight adjustment runs automatically during a maintenance pass (e.g., triggered by known gc, or lazily on session close/expiry). No manual 'known reinforce' command required. 2. Action-after-recall signal: edges to entries that were recalled AND subsequently acted on get weight boost. 3. Non-usage signal: edges to entries recalled but never acted on across N+ sessions get weight decay. 4. Edge weights stay bounded 0-1. 5. Reinforcement is idempotent (processing same session data twice produces same result). 6. Tests cover each signal type and the combined algorithm. 7. Zero user/LLM interaction required — fully automatic.
close_reason: Merged PR #19: Reinforce() method on Engine, action-after-recall pattern detection, GC integration, idempotent processing

title: Use ObservedAt for freshness instead of CreatedAt
description: Freshness/recency scoring in query/vector.go:64 computes temporal decay from entry.CreatedAt. But the model has an explicit Freshness.ObservedAt field for tracking when an entry was last observed/re-verified. An entry created a year ago but re-verified yesterday should be treated as fresh. Using CreatedAt conflates age with staleness, penalizing re-confirmed knowledge. Location: query/vector.go:64
acceptance_criteria: Freshness calculation uses entry.Freshness.ObservedAt when non-zero, falling back to CreatedAt. Tests verify that a re-observed old entry scores higher on freshness than a never-re-observed entry of the same age.

title: Prefix root scopes with / in scope tree display
description: The scope tree injected at session start shows all scopes across all projects in a flat hierarchy. This makes it unclear which scopes belong to the current project vs other projects stored in the same knowledge graph. Since we already use / as the escape syntax for cross-project scope references (e.g. known recall --scope /services-runtime), we should reuse that convention in the scope tree display. Each root scope should be prefixed with / to denote it as a project root.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Scope tree output prefixes each root scope with / (e.g. /known, /services-runtime)
2. The / prefix convention is documented in CLAUDE.md and plugin instructions
3. Skills that reference scopes explain the / prefix meaning
4. Existing scope tree generation code updated universally (CLI output, plugin hook injection, etc.)
close_reason: Added / prefix to root scopes in printTree() display. Updated test assertions and 7 documentation files to explain the convention: / marks project boundaries in scope tree, use bare names within your own project, / prefix only needed for cross-project access.

title: FTS5 full-text search for SQLite backend
description:
Add BM25-based full-text search via SQLite FTS5 to complement vector similarity search. This is a natural addition when moving to SQLite and actually improves on PostgreSQL's built-in ts_rank (which lacks IDF weighting).

Design:
- Create FTS5 virtual table mirroring entries content+scope: CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE entries_fts USING fts5(content, scope, content=entries, content_rowid=rowid)
- Keep in sync via triggers on entries table (INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE)
- Add SearchText(ctx, query string, opts) method to EntryRepo
- Hybrid search becomes: vector results UNION text results, deduplicated, re-ranked
- FTS5 bm25() provides relevance ranking automatically
- Supports: boolean operators, phrase search, prefix search, NEAR proximity

Benefits for known:
- Catches exact keyword matches that vector search misses (e.g., specific function names, error codes, UUIDs)
- BM25 ranking is genuinely better than PostgreSQL's ts_rank for relevance
- Zero additional dependencies — FTS5 is compiled into all SQLite drivers

Only implement for the SQLite backend initially. PostgreSQL backend can add pg_trgm or ts_vector later if needed.
design:
## Implementation Plan

### Architecture
FTS5 plugs into the existing two-tier architecture (storage → query → CLI):

1. **Storage layer**: New `SearchText()` on EntryRepo, FTS5 virtual table + triggers
2. **Query layer**: New `SearchText()` on Engine, plus update `SearchHybrid()` to optionally combine vector+text
3. **CLI layer**: New `--text` flag on search/recall to toggle text-only mode; `--hybrid` already exists but currently only means vector+graph — extend to include text

### Key Design Decisions

**Score normalization**: FTS5 bm25() returns negative scores (more negative = more relevant). Normalize to 0-1 scale: `score = 1.0 / (1.0 + abs(rank))`. This makes text scores composable with vector scores.

**Sync strategy**: Triggers (not application-level sync). FTS5 content-sync tables handle INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE automatically via triggers in the migration. This is zero-maintenance and atomic.

**Interface change**: Add `SearchText(ctx, query, scope, limit) ([]SimilarityResult, error)` to EntryRepo. Reuses SimilarityResult (Entry + Distance) — Distance holds raw BM25 rank. Postgres backend returns ErrNotImplemented initially.

**Hybrid combination**: In query.Engine.SearchHybrid, optionally run both SearchVector + SearchText, deduplicate by ID, take best score per entry, then graph-expand. Controlled by a new `TextWeight` field on HybridOptions (0.0 = vector only, default).

### Migration (007_fts5.sql)
- CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE entries_fts USING fts5(title, content, scope UNINDEXED, content=entries, content_rowid=rowid)
- Triggers: ai (after insert), ad (after delete), au (after update) on entries
- Backfill: INSERT INTO entries_fts(rowid, title, content, scope) SELECT rowid, title, content, scope FROM entries

### File Changes
1. `storage/sqlite/migrations/007_fts5.sql` — migration
2. `storage/storage.go` — add SearchText to EntryRepo
3. `storage/sqlite/entry.go` — implement SearchText (two-pass like SearchSimilar)
4. `storage/postgres/entry.go` — stub returning ErrNotImplemented
5. `query/query.go` — add TextOptions, SearchText method, TextWeight on HybridOptions
6. `query/text.go` — new file: Engine.SearchText implementation
7. `query/hybrid.go` — extend SearchHybrid to merge text results when TextWeight > 0
8. `cmd/search.go` — add --text flag
9. `cmd/recall.go` — add --text flag, wire into hybrid options
10. `storage/sqlite/sqlite_test.go` — FTS5 tests
acceptance_criteria: 1. FTS5 virtual table created alongside entries table in SQLite migrations. 2. Triggers keep FTS5 index in sync on INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE. 3. SearchText() method on EntryRepo returns entries matching full-text query with BM25 scores. 4. Boolean operators work: 'auth AND token', 'auth OR session', 'auth NOT cookie'. 5. Phrase search works: '"bearer token"'. 6. Prefix search works: 'auth*'. 7. Hybrid search combines vector + FTS5 results with deduplication. 8. FTS5 results include BM25 relevance score. 9. 'known search --hybrid' uses both vector and text matching. 10. Performance: FTS5 query on 10K entries completes in <50ms.
notes: SQLite FTS5 is complete (migration 007, SearchText, triggers, hybrid fusion via RRF). PostgreSQL tsvector/tsquery implementation still needed — the Postgres SearchText stub returns an error. SearchHybrid degrades gracefully when text search is unavailable, so this doesn't block the Postgres backend.

title: Design testbed to show tool effectiveness
description: Create an evaluation testbed that demonstrates known's effectiveness for real-world agent use cases. Current tests verify correctness of individual components but don't measure retrieval quality or demonstrate value. Need evaluation metrics (precision@k, MRR), realistic workload generation, and agent effectiveness scenarios. GitHub issue #14.
design:
## Implementation Plan

### Approach: Evaluation harness + realistic workload + demo scenario

### Component 1: Evaluation Metrics (test/eval/)
- eval.go — metrics functions: Precision@K, MRR, NDCG
- Labeled dataset format: query → expected entry IDs with relevance grades
- Runner that executes queries against a populated database and reports metrics

### Component 2: Workload Generator (test/eval/workload/)
- Generate entries from Go stdlib package docs or similar corpus
- Establish ground-truth edges based on import relationships
- Scale: 1K, 5K, 10K entry configs
- Populate via known's API (not CLI) for speed

### Component 3: Benchmarks (test/eval/bench_test.go)
- Search latency at 1K/5K/10K entries
- Memory usage profiling
- Embedding throughput
- Compare hugot vs ollama embedding quality on same queries

### Component 4: Effectiveness Demo (test/eval/demo/)
- Scenario: agent discovers codebase, stores knowledge, then answers questions
- Measure: how many questions answered correctly with recall vs without
- Output: markdown report with metrics

### Design Decisions
- Build on existing acceptance test infrastructure (in-memory SQLite)
- Use deterministic embeddings for reproducibility where possible
- Keep evaluation separate from unit/integration tests (own directory)
- Results stored as JSON for trending over time

### Dependencies
- Partially benefits from known-cr5 (recall filtering) but not blocked by it
acceptance_criteria: 1. Evaluation harness exists with precision@k and MRR metrics against labeled datasets. 2. Realistic workload generator populates 1K+ entries from real codebase semantics. 3. Benchmark suite measures search latency and memory at scale. 4. At least one end-to-end scenario demonstrates agent recall effectiveness (with vs without known). 5. Results are reproducible and can detect regressions.

title: Recall formatting duplicated between recall.go and output.go
description: recallByScope in cmd/recall.go:98 duplicates the entry formatting logic from PrintRecallResults in cmd/output.go:163. If recall format changes, both locations must be updated in lockstep. Should share formatting code.
acceptance_criteria: Single source of truth for recall entry formatting. No duplicated format strings.

title: Claude Code plugin packaging
description: Package Known as a Claude Code plugin for the plugin marketplace. Currently Known scaffolds skills/hooks into .claude/ via 'known init'. The plugin format requires a specific directory layout with .claude-plugin/plugin.json manifest, skills/, and hooks/ directories. Most content already exists — this is primarily a restructuring and packaging effort.
design:
Plugin follows the beads plugin pattern exactly:
- plugin.json with inline hooks (not separate hooks/ dir)
- commands/ with YAML frontmatter markdown files
- skills/known/SKILL.md as the main guidance doc
- Marketplace manifest at repo root .claude-plugin/marketplace.json

Key decisions:
- Hooks inline in plugin.json (matches beads pattern, simpler)
- Commands not skills for the 4 user-facing operations (remember/recall/forget/search)
- SKILL.md is the auto-loaded guidance context, commands are the slash-invokable actions
- known init kept as-is for standalone users (no code changes)
- Self-hosted marketplace in same repo via .claude-plugin/marketplace.json
acceptance_criteria:
1. Plugin directory exists with valid plugin.json manifest
2. Skills load correctly via 'claude --plugin-dir ./plugin'
3. SessionStart hook fires and injects scope tree
4. CLAUDE.md guidance references plugin-namespaced skills (/known:*)
5. 'known init' still works for standalone (non-plugin) users
6. Plugin can be installed from a marketplace source
close_reason: Plugin packaging complete. Created plugin/ directory with plugin.json manifest, 4 commands (remember/recall/forget/search), SKILL.md guidance, and marketplace.json for self-hosted distribution. Only remaining work is known-jjz (GitHub Releases for prebuilt binaries) which is a P3 follow-up.

title: Unit tests for project config integration in loadAppConfig
description: loadAppConfig now has a complex resolution chain (flag > env > project .known.yaml > global config > defaults) for DSN, scope, and other config. Unit tests need to cover: DSN resolution priority, ScopeRoot from project vs global scope_root, DefaultScope derivation, MaxContentLength/SearchThreshold/DefaultTTL override cascade from project config. Use temp dirs with .known.yaml files and mock/reset Viper state between tests. Also test expandHome helper.
close_reason: Added cmd/config_test.go with TestExpandHome (5 cases) and TestLoadAppConfig (23 cases) covering DSN priority, scope root, max content length, search threshold, default TTL, error paths, and flag passthrough. All pass.

title: Link UX needs iteration
description: Linking entries via CLI is error-prone for LLMs — they frequently fail to get the syntax right on first try. The link command UX needs to be more forgiving and intuitive. GH#25.
acceptance_criteria: 1. LLM agents can successfully link entries on first attempt >80% of the time. 2. Error messages for malformed link commands suggest the correct syntax. 3. Common link patterns (type:id) are well-documented in help text.
notes: Improved error messages: usage now shows valid edge types with examples; edge type validation error lists valid types. Resolution still uses resolveEntry() for fuzzy matching.
close_reason: Added comprehensive usage help with examples and valid edge types. Error on invalid edge type now lists valid options. Further iteration can happen based on agent feedback.

title: Raise recall default similarity threshold from 0.3 to 0.4+
description: recall --threshold defaults to 0.3, which lets in noisy, low-relevance entries. At 0.3 similarity the results include entries that are only tangentially related to the query. Raising the default to 0.4 or 0.5 would tighten relevance and reduce context pollution. Users who want broader recall can still pass --threshold 0.3 explicitly.
acceptance_criteria:
1. recall default threshold is raised (0.4 or 0.5)
2. search default threshold is also reviewed for consistency
3. Existing --threshold flag still overrides
4. Real-world queries return fewer, more relevant results
close_reason: Raised default threshold from 0.3 to 0.4 for both recall and search. Also wired app.Config.SearchThreshold to the flag defaults — previously the config field existed but was never read by any command.

title: Fix Duration.UnmarshalJSON to validate quoted string input
description: Duration.UnmarshalJSON in model/entry.go:60 slices data[1:len(data)-1] assuming JSON string quotes, but doesn't verify data[0]=='"'. A JSON number like 3600 or null would produce garbage input to time.ParseDuration with a confusing error.
acceptance_criteria: UnmarshalJSON validates data starts/ends with double quotes. Returns clear error for non-string JSON values. Test covers number and null inputs.

title: Tag and publish v0.1.0
description: Once CI and release automation are in place, tag the first release as v0.1.0 and push the tag to trigger the release workflow.
acceptance_criteria:
1. v0.1.0 tag exists and is pushed to origin
2. GitHub Release for v0.1.0 is created with binaries and changelog
3. 'go install github.com/dpoage/known/cmd/known@v0.1.0' works

title: Add 'move' command to re-scope entries
description: No way to change an entry's scope without delete+re-add, which loses version history and re-embeds. Need 'known move <id> --scope <new>' that updates scope in place. Bulk variant for moving all entries between scopes also useful. GH#28.
acceptance_criteria: 1. 'known move <id> --scope <new>' updates scope preserving ID, history, and embedding. 2. Bulk 'known move --from-scope X --to-scope Y' moves all matching entries. 3. Tests cover single and bulk moves.

title: Mistral embedding adapter
description:
Add a Mistral-specific embedder implementation. Mistral's API is close to OpenAI-compatible but has differences:

- Endpoint: POST https://api.mistral.ai/v1/embeddings (similar to OpenAI)
- Auth: Authorization: Bearer <key> (same as OpenAI)
- Request body is mostly OpenAI-compatible but with different model names
- Models: mistral-embed (1024 dims)
- Batch size limits differ from OpenAI
- Usage stats in response have a different shape

The openai-compatible adapter may already work for basic Mistral use. This task should first verify what breaks with the existing openai-compatible adapter pointing at Mistral's API, then only add a dedicated adapter if there are real incompatibilities. If it works as-is, close this as not-needed and add Mistral to the docs as an openai-compatible provider.

Config: KNOWN_EMBEDDER=mistral, KNOWN_EMBED_API_KEY=<key>, KNOWN_EMBED_MODEL=mistral-embed
acceptance_criteria: 1. First: test existing openai-compatible adapter against Mistral API (https://api.mistral.ai/v1/embeddings). 2. If it works: close this issue, document Mistral as an openai-compatible provider in README/config docs. 3. If it breaks: implement MistralEmbedder with fixes for the specific incompatibilities found. 4. Document any batch size limit differences. 5. Default model: mistral-embed (1024 dims). 6. Unit test with mocked HTTP if a dedicated adapter is needed.

title: Tag QoL: label list command, docs, and completion
description: Labels are a first-class feature but have poor discoverability. No way to enumerate labels, no shell completion, no documentation in scaffold CLAUDE.md.
acceptance_criteria: 1. 'known label list' command enumerates all labels in the DB. 2. Shell completion suggests label values for --label flags. 3. Scaffold CLAUDE.md documents label usage. 4. Search --label post-filter gap documented or fixed.
close_reason: Added LabelLister interface + implementations for both SQLite and Postgres. New 'known label list' command. Dynamic label completion for --label flags across all shells. Scaffold CLAUDE.md updated with labels documentation.

title: LLM Performance & Sane Defaults
description: Make known actually improve LLM performance rather than pollute context. Covers: zero-config embedded embeddings, LLM-optimized output, acceptance tests, and sane defaults (content limits, auto-TTL, threshold floor, auto-dedup).
close_reason: All children complete: Go-native ONNX embedder (zero-config), dedicated recall command, SQLite acceptance tests (7 scenarios), and sane defaults (content limits, auto-TTL, threshold floor, dedup). Known now has a working LLM-optimized retrieval pipeline with no external dependencies.

title: Go-native ONNX embedder (zero-config default)
description: Replace Ollama as default embedder with a Go-native ONNX runtime embedding. Bundle all-MiniLM-L6-v2 (80MB, 384 dimensions). known add should just work with zero external dependencies. Keep Ollama and OpenAI-compatible as opt-in upgrades via KNOWN_EMBEDDER=ollama|openai-compatible.
design:
Research findings (Feb 2026):

RECOMMENDED: hugot (github.com/knights-analytics/hugot, 565 stars, Apache-2.0)
- Pure Go backend via GoMLX — no CGo for default path
- Feature extraction pipeline for sentence embeddings
- Auto-downloads models from HuggingFace
- API: NewGoSession() -> NewPipeline(session, featureExtractionConfig) -> RunPipeline([]string)
- Pure Go works best with small batches (~32) — perfect for single-entry known add
- Optional CGo backends (ORT, XLA) via build tags for perf-critical use
- RISK: still needs libtokenizers.a (Rust static lib) for tokenization. Verify build experience.

ALTERNATIVE: clems4ever/all-minilm-l6-v2-go (12 stars)
- Embeds model in binary (~90MB), requires onnxruntime installed
- Less mature but more self-contained

REJECTED: onnxruntime-purego (8 stars, 'unstable' warning)
REJECTED: pure Go inference (gorgonia/onnx-go — not production ready)

Implementation plan:
1. Add hugot as embed backend type: 'hugot' (new default)
2. On first use, auto-download all-MiniLM-L6-v2 to ~/.known/models/
3. Keep ollama and openai-compatible as opt-in overrides
4. Build tags: default build gets hugot+GoMLX (pure Go). -tags ORT gets onnxruntime perf.
acceptance_criteria: 1. known add 'test content' works with fresh install, no Ollama, no env vars. 2. Embedding quality: cosine similarity between 'Go is a programming language' and 'Golang is a compiled language' > 0.7. 3. Latency: single embedding < 100ms on commodity hardware. 4. KNOWN_EMBEDDER=ollama still works. 5. go test ./embed/... passes.
notes: Latency benchmarks (model cached): Init=250ms, first embed=98ms, cold-start-to-result=348ms, warm=69ms/embed. Pure Go (CGO_ENABLED=0) confirmed working. Model downloaded to ~/.known/models/ on first use (~80MB). Semantic quality: Go/Golang similarity=0.77, Go/weather=0.05. hugot v0.6.2, GoMLX backend, all-MiniLM-L6-v2 384-dim.
close_reason: Implemented HugotEmbedder as new default (KNOWN_EMBEDDER=hugot). Pure Go via GoMLX — no CGo, no external services. Model: all-MiniLM-L6-v2, 384 dims, auto-downloaded to ~/.known/models/. Cold start 348ms, 69ms/embed warm. Ollama and openai-compatible preserved as opt-in overrides. All 25 unit tests + integration test pass.

title: Dedicated 'recall' command for LLM retrieval
description:
Add a dedicated 'known recall' command purpose-built for LLM tool-use consumption. NOT a flag on search — a separate command with its own hardcoded defaults optimized for minimal context pollution.

Design (informed by research across MCP, mem0, Letta, Zep):
- Two parameters: query (required), scope (optional, default root)  
- Hardcoded: limit=5, threshold=0.3, hybrid search on, recency_weight=0.1
- Plain text output: content + [scope] + (confidence, source). No IDs, no scores, no JSON overhead.
- Zero results: 'No matching knowledge found.'
- No preamble.

This is the command that MCP tool definitions and LLM function-calling integrations should target.
design:
Output format per entry:

[scope.path] (confidence, source: reference)
The actual knowledge content here.

Separated by blank lines. Entries pre-sorted by relevance (best first). No JSON unless combined with --json flag for programmatic consumers that still want minimal output.

Implementation: new cmd/recall.go calling Engine.SearchHybrid with fixed options. ~50 lines. Shares no output code with search — its own minimal formatter.

MCP tool definition for future integration:
{name: 'recall', inputSchema: {query: string, scope?: string}, annotations: {readOnlyHint: true}}
acceptance_criteria: 1. known recall 'auth tokens' returns plain text with content+scope+confidence+source only. 2. No IDs, scores, timestamps, versions, or embedding data in output. 3. Default limit is 5, threshold is 0.3. 4. --scope narrows to scope subtree. 5. Token count for 5 results < 40% of equivalent 'known search --json' output. 6. 'No matching knowledge found.' on zero results.
close_reason: Recall command implemented: cmd/recall.go with hardcoded hybrid search (limit=5, threshold=0.3, recency=0.1), plain-text LLM-optimized output via PrintRecallResults, --scope flag with auto-derived default.

title: SQLite-based acceptance tests
description: End-to-end acceptance tests against SQLite (no Docker required). Tests exercise the full round-trip through the storage.Backend interface: add entries with embeddings, search by similarity, link entries with edges, traverse graph, detect conflicts, gc expired entries, export/import round-trip, dedup behavior, and scope isolation. These validate that the system works correctly as an integrated whole — not just that individual repo methods work. Uses in-memory SQLite (:memory:) for speed.
design: No Docker/testcontainers needed. Use in-memory SQLite directly via sqlite.New(ctx, ":memory:"). Each test gets a fresh DB instance. Test scenarios exercise multi-step workflows through the Backend interface, not individual repos. Tag with //go:build integration to keep separate from unit tests. Place in test/acceptance/ directory.
acceptance_criteria: 1. go test -tags=integration ./test/acceptance/... passes against real pgvector. 2. Tests complete in <60s. 3. CI can run them (Docker available). 4. Each test is independent (own schema or truncate between tests). 5. At least 7 test scenarios covering the full CRUD+search+graph lifecycle.
close_reason: 7 SQLite-based acceptance tests in test/acceptance/acceptance_test.go — AddAndSearch, GraphTraversal, ConflictDetection, TTLAndGC, Dedup, ScopeIsolation, EdgeCascadeOnDelete. All pass against in-memory SQLite, no Docker needed. Uses //go:build integration tag.

title: Sane defaults: content limits, auto-TTL, threshold floor, auto-dedup
description:
Implement four sane defaults that prevent context pollution:

1. **Content length limit**: Max 4096 chars per entry. Reject longer content with a clear error suggesting the user break it into atomic pieces. Configurable via config but the default enforces discipline.

2. **Auto-TTL by source type**: conversation=7d, manual=90d, file/url=no expiry. Applied at add time when no explicit --ttl is given. Configurable defaults in ~/.known/config.yaml.

3. **Similarity threshold floor**: Default --threshold=0.3 on search/recall so garbage matches never reach the LLM. Currently defaults to 0. The --threshold flag still overrides.

4. **Auto-dedup on add**: If content_hash matches an existing entry in the same scope, update the existing entry (bump timestamps, update source) instead of creating a duplicate. Use the existing CreateOrUpdate storage method.
design:
Implementation plan:

1. Content limit: Add MaxContentLength to AppConfig (default 4096). Check in runAdd and runUpdate before embedding. Also validate in model.Entry.Validate() as a soft check.

2. Auto-TTL: Add DefaultTTL map[SourceType]time.Duration to AppConfig. In runAdd, if no --ttl flag and source type has a default, apply it. Config file example:
   default_ttl:
     conversation: 168h
     manual: 2160h

3. Threshold floor: Change VectorOptions default from 0 to 0.3. Update runSearch to use 0.3 as default instead of 0. The recall command inherits this.

4. Auto-dedup: In runAdd, after computing content hash, check if entry with same hash+scope exists. If so, use Update (preserving ID, bumping version) instead of Create. Print a message indicating it was a dedup update vs new entry.
acceptance_criteria: 1. known add with >4096 char content returns clear error. 2. known add --source-type=conversation without --ttl gets 7d TTL. 3. known search 'query' with no --threshold returns nothing below 0.3 score. 4. Adding duplicate content in same scope updates existing entry, not creates new. 5. All four defaults are configurable in ~/.known/config.yaml. 6. Existing tests still pass.
close_reason: All four sane defaults implemented: MaxContentLength=4096 with validation in model+add+update, auto-TTL by source type (conversation=7d, manual=90d), search threshold default=0.3, auto-dedup via CreateOrUpdate on content_hash+scope. All configurable via project .known.yaml or global ~/.known/config.yaml.

title: Add 'remember' as CLI alias for 'add'
description: Add remember as alias for add command so agents using the /remember skill can call 'known remember' directly.
acceptance_criteria: 1. 'known remember ...' works identically to 'known add ...'. 2. remember appears in --help. 3. All tests pass.
close_reason: Added 'remember' as case alias in root.go dispatch, also added to needsEmbedder check and usage text.

title: Fix serializeFloat32 return signature — error is always nil
description: serializeFloat32 in storage/sqlite/vec.go:9 returns ([]byte, error) but never returns a non-nil error. Callers check for errors that can never happen. Misleading signature.
acceptance_criteria: serializeFloat32 returns []byte only (no error). Callers updated. Or: document why error return is intentional.

title: Reduce per-query allocations in SearchSimilar
description: Each SearchSimilar call allocates a new []float32 per candidate via deserializeFloat32. For 10K candidates with 768-dim embeddings, that's 10K allocations totaling ~30MB that immediately becomes garbage. Improvements: (1) Operate directly on the byte blob — compute dot product and norms by reading float32 values in-place from the []byte without deserializing to a separate slice. binary.LittleEndian.Uint32 + math.Float32frombits is cheap and avoids the allocation. (2) If deserialization is kept, use a sync.Pool of reusable buffers. (3) Pre-allocate the candidates slice with an estimated capacity (e.g. from a COUNT query or a reasonable default).
acceptance_criteria: Benchmark shows reduced allocations per query (check -benchmem output). Allocation count for 10K candidates should drop from ~10K to near zero.

title: Fix recall-before-exploring soft loop
description: CLAUDE.md says 'recall before exploring' and escape hatch checks for scope tree presence (wrong signal). A project can have scopes but zero entries, leading LLMs into: see scopes -> recall -> nothing -> try lower threshold -> nothing -> stuck. Need clean exit path decoupled from scope presence.
acceptance_criteria: 1. CLAUDE.md guidance decouples 'should I recall' from 'scopes exist' 2. Explicit 'if recall returns nothing, proceed normally' at CLAUDE.md level 3. No instruction chain leads to a dead end on empty graphs
close_reason: Rewrote CLAUDE.md and plugin SKILL.md to decouple recall trigger from scope presence. New wording: 'If recall returns no results, proceed normally — the graph may not have entries yet.' Added explicit 'If known is unavailable, ignore these instructions' escape hatch. Recall skill doc step 3 now says proceed normally instead of chaining to search.

title: Set up GitHub Releases with prebuilt binaries
description:
Users should be able to install known without needing a Go toolchain. The dependency tree is heavy (ONNX, tokenizers, Docker SDK, etc.) and may include CGo dependencies that cause build failures on some systems. Prebuilt binaries via GitHub Releases solve both problems.

This involves:
1. Tagging a first version (v0.1.0)
2. Setting up GoReleaser (or similar) to produce cross-platform binaries
3. Adding a GitHub Actions workflow to build and publish releases on tag push
4. Documenting installation options (go install + binary download)
acceptance_criteria:
- A tagged release (e.g. v0.1.0) exists with prebuilt binaries for linux/amd64, linux/arm64, darwin/amd64, darwin/arm64 at minimum
- A GitHub Actions workflow automatically builds and publishes binaries when a version tag is pushed
- `go install github.com/dpoage/known/cmd/known@v0.1.0` also works
- README or docs include installation instructions for both methods

title: Restructure CLAUDE.md/SKILL.md for minimal always-on context
description: Always-on context (CLAUDE.md + plugin SKILL.md) is ~4000 tokens with massive redundancy. LLMs on fresh windows get overwhelmed with guidance instead of focusing on the user's task. Need to restructure to behavioral-first, reference-second, and eliminate duplication between always-on layer and per-skill docs.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Always-on context (CLAUDE.md or plugin SKILL.md) is under 300 tokens 2. Skills table and behavioral rules appear first 3. Reference material (source types, provenance, flags, CLI commands) lives only in per-skill docs 4. Scaffold and plugin paths are mutually exclusive or deduplicated 5. Fallback instruction for 'known not available' is present
close_reason: Restructured plugin SKILL.md (213 words/~280 tokens) and scaffold CLAUDE.md (175 words/~230 tokens) to behavioral-first format. Removed all reference material (source types, provenance, flags, CLI commands tables) — now lives only in per-skill docs. Added 'known unavailable' fallback. Skills table is first section after one-line description.

title: Pluggable scaffold adapters
description: Refactor scaffold.Install() so the target directory, instructions filename, hook format, and skill layout are parameterized by an adapter interface. Currently everything is hardcoded to Claude Code (.claude/ dir, CLAUDE.md, settings.json hooks). OpenClaw uses a nearly identical skill format (SKILL.md with YAML frontmatter) but different directories (~/.openclaw/skills), instructions file (AGENTS.md), and hook config (openclaw.json). Ship adapters for: claude (current behavior), openclaw, and generic (AGENTS.md + plain skills/). Wire into known init --agent <name>.
acceptance_criteria:
- Adapter interface defined with methods for: target dir, instructions filename, hook config format, skill directory layout
- claude adapter reproduces current behavior exactly (no regression)
- openclaw adapter emits OpenClaw-compatible skill structure and AGENTS.md
- generic adapter emits AGENTS.md + framework-agnostic skills/ directory
- known init --agent <name> selects adapter; default is claude
- Scaffold templates are shared where possible (skill content is the same, only wrapper differs)

title: Fix silent zero time.Time on parseTime failure
description: parseTime in storage/sqlite/scope.go:184 silently returns time.Time{} (year 0001) when both time.Parse attempts fail. This causes entries with corrupted timestamps to appear maximally stale, affecting freshness labels and recency scoring in queries.
acceptance_criteria: parseTime returns an error or logs a warning when both parse attempts fail. Callers handle the failure case.

title: Fix broken TestRunCompleteLight_Scopes test
description: cmd/completion_test.go TestRunCompleteLight_Scopes seeds scopes into one in-memory DB then opens a fresh :memory: DB for the actual test, so it never tests scope listing output. The test is effectively a no-op. Comment in test acknowledges this.
acceptance_criteria: Test uses a shared DB instance (file-based temp DB or same :memory: handle) so seeded scopes are visible during completion.

title: recall output loses metadata from flag-parsing bug
description: The recall command's plain-text output shows scope, confidence, and source-type. When entries were added with flags after content (silently ignored), the output shows incorrect defaults (root/inferred/manual) instead of intended values. This is a downstream consequence of the flag-parsing bug but also highlights that recall has no way to filter by scope, confidence, or source-type.
acceptance_criteria:
1. After flag-parsing bug is fixed, recall output correctly reflects entry metadata
2. recall supports -scope flag to filter results by scope
close_reason: Fixed by pflag migration — recall -scope flag now works regardless of argument order. No separate fix needed.
